Kantutan is [Luzon/Provincial] Makiling Botanic Gardens Plant of the Month for February 👃 Get ready for a plant that challenges your senses! This month, we're featuring Kantutan (Paederia foetida L.) from the [Luzon/Provincial] Makiling Botanic Gardens – and yes, it lives up to its "stinky" scientific name! 😉🌿 Don't let the strong sulfuric smell fool you when its leaves are crushed! This vine is a plant of fascinating contradictions: its genus name means "opals" (for beauty!), and it boasts striking dark purple and grayish-white flowers. Across Asia, Kantutan has been a culinary staple (boiled leaves, noodles, soups!) and a powerhouse in traditional medicine, used for everything from treating gas and fever to soothing snake bites and even preventing toothaches! It's a reminder that beauty (and utility!) comes in many forms, even the stinky ones! Come discover the surprising story of Kantutan at the Makiling Botanic Gardens! Read the full article here👉 Watch and listen to the video instead 👉 📸by For. [REDACTED_NAME] Limpiada & For. Analyn Malayba

[Metro Manila] Ang dami kong nakikitang posts about sa pambabash ng [Metro Manila] students sa other UP campuses. While unnecessary ito, let’s be honest here, there is at least some sort of truth of to that. Kung magpapakatotoo tayo, 3 campus lang naman sa UP ang masasabing top-performing. 1. [Metro Manila] - given na ‘to dahil ito ang flagship campus at ito ang may pinakamaraming degree program options, kaya mas maraming research output at impact sa different fields na nagagawa. Magaling sa lahat ng fields na available mapa-science, business, humanities, at social sciences. Nandito rin ang karamihan sa national centers, tho given dahil malaki ang campus at nandito ang admin. 2. UPM - health sciences. Nasa kanila rin ang PGH. At based sa info ay sila rin ang pinaka-strict sa pagtanggap ng students. Kinulang lang sa budget talaga para sa pagpapaayos ng buildings at pagpuksa sa mga daga nilang kasing laki na ng pusa hahaha. 3. [Luzon/Provincial] - uso ang [Metro Manila] vs [Luzon/Provincial] pero di naman natin matatanggi na nasa top din talaga ang [Luzon/Provincial]. Malaki ang impact nila sa agricultural sciences, natural science, at environmental science. At par din sa [Metro Manila] ang engineering at science programs nila, kinulang lang talaga sa humanities and social sciences hahaha. Masyado lang silang mabait sa pagtanggap sa students kaya nape-perceive ng mga taga-[Metro Manila] na mas superior sila. At kung mapapansin nyo, nasa 3 campus lang na yan ang majority ng mga national centers, at silang 3 rin ang nakakatanggap ng karamihan ng budget for infrastructure and resources. This post is not intended to degrade the rest of the UP campuses, but a wake up call to the admin here in [Metro Manila] na gawing pantay pantay ang resource and budget na binibigay sa bawat campus para matigil na ang so-called “campus discrimination”.

[Luzon/Provincial] To N, i hope good things find you. not that i consider myself bad, i guess fate was just feeling a little silly when it decided that we should meet. might have been my delusions, but it even took it as far as sending me some foreshadowing. some of which are just too much to be a coincidence, and i h*ted every part of that. i h*te that i wanted you so bad whilst knowing that i can never be yours. i wish i could be casual about all this, i really thought i could, but man it was really hard. geiko, im sorry. hindi ko kayang magmahal nang tahimik, if i can't have it now then maybe it wasn't mine at all and i never deserved it in the first place. i know these feelings shall pass, but i feel too much that i stray away from my focus. how can i devote myself to loving you if i am unable to love myself? too much thoughts swirling through my head but really, ang bottomline lang naman talaga nito is that i over romanticized everything. i guess ako lang naman yung nagbigay ng meaning, nagtanim ng hope sa sarili ko. gusto ko maiyak rn but i can't. i miss you so much that it almost feels wrong?? - taong nagbrowse ng the unsent project and couldn't find an entry about "N" bc of how uncommon their name is and so they put one themselves while browsing their own name (it's most likely not them who sent this one on the image but hey, one is allowed to dream) [REDACTED_NAME] 25, 2:37:35 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] A mini guide for incoming freshies This is a list of pages I find useful for my everyday Elbi Life * [Luzon/Provincial] Student Help desk- mostly for questions from currently enrolled students. (Incoming freshies mag search button at Google maps muna kayo before posting here awa nalang.) *[Luzon/Provincial] Freedom Wall- most appropriate alternative place if gusto niyo magtanong ng what to expect IMO, para hindi flooded yung kabila ng mga not so urgent questions niyo. *[Luzon/Provincial] Housemates- dorm recos, reviews etc. Mostly for non-UP owned dorms. You can also ask for transients here etc. *[Luzon/Provincial] Office of Student Housing- If you want to apply/inquire for dorms INSIDE [Luzon/Provincial] and UP owned *[Luzon/Provincial] - yung verified page, for official announcements *UP Shiftees and Transferees - how to shift, how to transfer etc. Pages to follow na mostly when you are current student na pinaka helpful *[Luzon/Provincial] DX AMIS Support Group (enrollment purposes) *[Luzon/Provincial] University Library (study place + verification of ID) *Respective student council pages *Elbi Lost and Found I probably missed a lot of other helpful pages that you may learn about from your ates and kuyas :) Some important reminders 1.Search Function exists 2.Google Maps exists 3.Follow the assigned schedule especially during enrollment season tapos magchecheck pa kayo ng class schedule niyo by accessing AMIS kahit di niyo pa naman maview. 4.Wearing ID Laces are completely normal 5.Pakiiwan Main Character Syndrome(if meron) niyo sa bahay niyo. Remember to be responsible and respectful adults befitting someone na pinapaaral ng buwis ng bayan. Congrats and Welcome to ELBI ! [REDACTED_NAME] 24, 3:37:21 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] hello po, i don’t know when this will get posted but i’d just like to share my thoughts on this. it’s been a few days since the passing of ms. alyssa, the [Metro Manila] usc councilor that was involved in defending negros 19. i don’t know her personally, but i’ve been having this ache in my chest about her passing. it pains me that several people have been calling her and the others involved names such as “corned beef” or saying na “deserve naman nila ‘yan” o “dagdagan pa, bakit hindi lahat?” this is so concerning. gan’to karami pa palang mamamayan ng pilipinas ang nabubulag-bulagan patungkol sa mga isyung napapaloob sa bansa natin. this is not bliss ignorance if they themselves choose to close their mind and set it on false narratives. i’ve been thinking for the past couple of days, “paano na ang pilipinas?” “may magagawa ba talaga ang puspusang panghahamig sa ganito kalalang sitwasyon?” and let me answer myself, yes. a small movement can create a big impact. kaya, let’s participate in rallies and use our voices to stand for what’s right, what’s just. hindi na para i-gag* pa tayo ng gobyerno, maging mulat tayo. kung hindi ka pa galit, kababayan, bakit? [REDACTED_NAME] 24, 12:53:31 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Alone in my 25th birthday But Its ok It's my birthday in a few hours, and I am alone. No family to speak to kasi my parents abandoned me and they have their own separate families now. No friends to call or invite. As in mag-isa talaga ako sa buhay ngayon. Well, at least malapit na maka-graduate. But I crave human connection, the last time I spoke to someone that was not about acads was when I went out in a date last February 12. From my perspective it went well and it was great, she even thanked me for that date but the day after that, I was ghosted and she blocked me... but its ok i love the silence am I can’t believe that am a happy that am alone. It is kind of bittersweet actually. To realize that I am getting older but still all alone. There's a weird satisfaction knowing I made it this far by myself at the same time what more could I have achieved if someone, just one person, believed in me and supported me? I think no one talks about male loneliness as much as they do about other m*ntal health challenges. Baka OA lang din ako kasi kinaya ko nga for so many years at nabubuhay naman ako. I just feel like may kulang sa akin kasi mag-isa lang ako. [REDACTED_NAME] 23, 10:55:08 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] minahal kita, 4 years. (carson?! dinaig 7 years?!) i loved you for four years, even though we were never really anything. kahit once a year lang tayo nagkakausap, and right after we meet, you disappear again like nothing ever happened. it was stupid of me to believe there could be something real between us. the last time we saw each other, it was the first time you held my hand, hugged me, and kissed me. and somehow, i already knew that was going to be the last. tanda ko pa rin yung araw na magkasama tayo, at sinabi mo sa’kin na hindi mo nakikita sarili mo na magkakaroon ng asawa. doon pa lang, alam ko na. i just chose to hope anyway (noon). pero ang sakit pa rin. not because i still want you, but because you left me confused and disrespected, like i was something you could pick up and drop whenever it was convenient for you. every time i see you, sumasakit dibdib ko—not out of love, but because i remember how easily you made me feel abandoned. i don’t want you anymore. i’m finally happy with my life, and i mean that. pero nandito pa rin yung galit at sakit, not because i miss you, but because i know i didn’t deserve the way you treated me. but still, thank you. you made me realize that i can love that long, that deeply. and if i was capable of loving the wrong person for four years, then i know i’ll be capable of loving the right one even better. maybe it’s my fault too. i was never fully authentic with you. i tried so hard to understand you, to like everything you liked, that i slowly lost the chance to show you who i really was. i wanted closure before, but now i see it for what it [Luzon/Provincial] The real closure is being okay with unfinished endings; may it be messy with a lot of words left unsaid. [Luzon/Provincial] Hi. Creator of Pansol Directory here. Heavy heart to say that it didn't succeed the way we aimed for. Hence, it will not be developed any further. I still believe in the idea, pero baka not for right now, or hindi talaga siya meant to be built. Ecstatic yung amount of support and reactions to the website, and genuinely thought na we could use that as traction moving forward. Highly appreciative to those who tried to help, and those who cooperated to get listed. Make use of this post as a postmortem ng Pansol Directory. I built the website in hopes of making it easier for everyone, both resort owners and renters (students, families, workers, etc). And I'd like to say I got us close to such a platform with things I can control. Pero naging highly dependent talaga yung success sa efforts ko on trying to onboard resort owners. And I knew that going in. Initially, I wanted na to go straight sa owners. I joined Pansol-posting groups na puro middlemen. Checking individual accounts, para madistinguish yung owners. Messaged them. Pero no traction. May magsshow ng interest, pero ghost on the details. After seeing that fail, nagtry na rin ako na magreach out sa Resort Pages, kasi typically an admin person (if not the owner) would be handling that. And good chances na baka mapasali. Still nothing. Up until na nagmessage na rin ako sa middlemen, in hopes na mai-connect sa owners. Kasi may GC sila, with other middlemen and the owner. (IIRC that's how their system works, dont quote me) Pero no success there. (Expected) And so, out of the hundreds of messages I sent out to various people/resorts. Only 2 yung successfully nagprovide ng kanilang details to be included sa Directory. I'd like to state here that their cooperation was highly appreciated and gave me hope early that I may have had something going on with the website. The first two resorts na makikita niyo sa Pansol Directory, those are the ones. If y'all are able to support them, please do. So yon, there's a lot to learn sa pagpapatakbo ng Pansol Directory, sa pagbuild ng website, sa pagmarket, pagbuild ng services. And won't stop moving forward. The site will stay up indefinitely. And I'll be moving on to other projects. I'd like to keep my services open para sa Elbi. If you've got a business you'd like me to build a website for, or you need a custom software solution. I'd like to help for free. Yes, for free. I want to focus on my portfolio, and building out services. Anyways, that's all. Piz out. [REDACTED_NAME] 17, 10:41:46 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Replying to [Luzon/Provincial] As a tutor, I can say that this extends to the understanding of the students about the material. It gives me the ick when I hear or see that the person I am teaching is using AI for studying. Kahit yung simpleng definition or explanation ng word or concept, gumagamit ng AI. Please lang, google search mo, read from multiple sources, then form your own synthesis. Even on the smallest of things, kailangan maexercise yung brain nyo. Kung ganyan kasi literally malilimutan mo kaagad yan kasi hindi naman yung own brain mo ang nagformulate ng explanation. Mas malala na lang talaga kapag ginagamit yung AI for complex topics, I swear. Kahit anong gaslight nyo na "it's just a tool," multiple, if not, all of the studies about topic has shown that using literally degrades your brain. Which is common sense naman kasi naooffload yung dapat na exercise for your brain. Oh you can't write eloquently? Then write poorly. For now. You'll get good at it. Eventually. What's the hurry? Bakit kailangan pabilisin lahat? May hinahabol ba? Take your f-ing time. Don't use it for brainstorming. Don't use it for paraphrasing. Don't use it for generating or editing images. Don't use it for generating sample problems. Don't use it for generating code. Don't use it for literally anything kung gusto mo maging functional na tao pa rin. You're depriving yourself of your potential. You are so much smarter and more capable than that thing. March 30, 11:43:22 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Matagal ko nang isinuko ang pagiging manunulat. Lagi kong iniisip na lipas na ang panahon na kaya ko pang mag sulat - at lubos akong naniwala noon na walang kariktan ang makapagpapabalik sa akin sa pagkuha ng aking papel at panulat. Pero nakilala kita. Mula nang una kitang makita, alam ko na agad sa sarili ko na ikaw ang magiging bida ng aking bawat sulatin. Detalyado kong uulit-ulitin ang alaala ko sayo: kung paanong sa unang lapat ng mata mo sa akin ay tila hinigit ng husto ang hininga ko, at mula noong sandaling iyon ay nagbago na ang ritmo ng tibok ng puso ko. Pilit na nakikisabay sa’yo, kahit na parang ang layo layo mo. Ngayong taon lang kita nakilala, ngunit ginulo at pinunan mo ang isip ko sa paraang mahihiling ko lang na magawa ko rin sa dapat kong aralin ngayong gabing ito. Hindi ko kayang mag-aral, at tinanggap ko na na nagpapanggap na lamang ako-buhat na hindi mapakali sa tindi ng nararamdaman para sa iyo. Alam mo ba, na ang interaksyon natin sa bawat linggo ang nagtutulak sa’kin tungo pa sa kasunod na linggo? Duda ako. Madalang mong pantayan ang tingin ko, at malapit na akong makumbinsi na ako ang pinaka hindi interesanteng bagay na nakita mo sa tanang buhay mo. Pero ayos lang. Sa sobrang pagkabaliw ko sa’yo, lahat ng kaya mong ibigay ay tatanggapin ko. Kung ang lakarin lamang mula sa nauna nating klase tungo sa kasunod ang oras na maibibigay mo sa akin, pahahalagan ko ito nang husto. Isusulat ko na lang siguro. Tatandaan kong maigi kung paano hipan ng hangin ang buhok mo kapag napapadpad tayo sa CPark, at kung gaano kabanayad ang halik ng araw sa moreno mong balat tuwing tanghaling tapat. Itatatak ko sa puso ko ang mga saglit na panahong hinahayaan mo kong makilala ka. Mula sa kung gaano ka mapili sa upuan mo sa tuwing mag-aaral ka, sa paborito mong ballpen na hindi mo mabitiw bitiwan, at sa kalendaryo mong iba’t iba ang laman. [Luzon/Provincial] I've never written sa freedom wall pero gusto ko lang mag vent. Ang hirap mag move on from unclear endings. It was hard for me to move on, from when we last spoke in Messenger in July all the way to September. I spent sleepless nights regretful of my actions and timing. I prayed every day that we could fix things. Alam ko naman I started it; there is no moment where I see a glimpse of you that I don't remember my mistake. Everytime we would meet, by sheer chance--like that one time we bumped into each other on the street--I could never tell if you were resenting me or longing to talk to me again. I would ask how you're doing, but your answers felt cold. I could never tell what you were thinking or how you felt. It was all so muddy and so unclear. I tried to make amends. I tried to clear things. I gave you space. I wanted to comfort you. I wanted to mourn with you. But what else was I supposed to do when time passes by? When things are unclear? When things were left to a "maybe"? Just keep waiting? How long? It seems like you didn't want to talk--even at the very least, clarify things. Is making the effort to communicate a one-way thing? I understand you were going through hell and that giving me a clear yes or no was the least of your priorities. But how long was I to wait? Am I wrong for realizing that no answer is an answer? It was September when I realized that there's no hearing from you. I decided to move on. I thought your silence meant you were telling me off. Is it wrong for me to move on? Is it wrong for me to find love, when I had moved on? I resolved in my heart to move on completely before pursuing anyone. I waited 4 months since September to make sure I had completely moved on. I hurts to see your friends giving me looks, kasi nagtataka ako ano sinasabi mo. Pero walang clarity eh and now we're both left guessing what either of us are up to. I h*te that I am probably the subject of gossip. I just wish we clarified things. I just wish we could have been more communicative. I'm not blaming you. I understand you're still mourning, busy, and on your last semester. I'm just sad that things had to be this way and had to be this way. I also shouldn't blame you because the absolute shit-fest of events that happened in your life all at once could break anyone. And I'm sorry for adding to it. I'm doing better and making efforts to be more sensitive. If by some sheer chance you know who this is, I just want to tell you, I'm grateful to have met you--I'm grateful to have known you. I admire your courage and your strength. I pray for you and your family when I can. I'm thankful for your gifts. You're thoughtful, beautiful, funny, meek, and full of joy; you touch the lives of the people you meet, in ways you don't realize. At least, that's what I caught in our short moment of dating. I hope the next man you meet will discover the beauty of you, make it flourish. I'm sorry for my timing. But I also cannot deny that communication is so important, and that to work through hard times is the most loving thing we can do. I genuinely believe that we could have been compatible, but I guess it just isn't now. To quote, from Alain de Bottom, "compatability is not the precondition of love, but compatability is the fruit of love." Perhaps our idea of love was not the same. Maybe in another lifetime. Anyway. That's it. I wish you the best March 27, 4:50:07 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Dear roommates, I just want to kindly ask for your consideration, especially during nighttime. Napapansin ko kasi na tuloy-tuloy yung pag-click ng mouse or laptop from night hanggang umaga, and nakakaistorbo siya sa tulog. Maaari naman sa labas ng room mag-aral. Basic decency na yun. May exams, quizzes, and lab works din kasi kami sa iisang araw and kulang na nga sa tulog, naaapektuhan pa lalo yung focus and performance sa classes especially sa recitation. Ang hirap na hindi ako makapagrecite and patuloy na napapansin iyon ng mga propesors mula simula ng semestre. Bilang estudyante, dapat makiramdam din ako sa mga propesors ko. Hindi na ako makapagpasa on the spot ng exercises dahil hindi na maayos cognition at vision ko sapagkat hindi maayos ang tulog ko dahil sa click ng mouse. Hindi ako makasagot ng maayos at ang hirap irecall dahil hindi ako nakatulog ng maayos kahit anong pilit ko. Upper batches na tayo and some are preparing for internship and graduation, so malaking bagay talaga yung maayos na pahinga especially tulog. Let us all check our privilege especially sa health. Okay? May mga estudyante na hindi kaya makapagfunction ng maayos kapag hindi nakakatulog ng maayos. If it works for you, then good for you, but consider others please. Hindi lang kinabukasan mo nakasalalay dito. Nakasalalay din dito kinabukasan ng mga karoommates mo. Mahirap na madelay dahil lang sa kulang sa tulog. Hindi rin pwedeng laging isang tao lang ang nag-aadjust. I hope we know the unspoken rules since we are adults na. May karoommate ka mula umaga hanggang gabi ang klase at puro exams, recitations, quizzes and lab works siya sa iisang araw and every week iyon. At alamkong may ganyan ka din. Sana we can all be more mindful and considerate of each other. Let’s help each other get through this para makapaginternship na at makagraduate please. March 24, 7:38:56 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] This is a response to [Luzon/Provincial]. Why ghosting is actually a healthy and normal thing. A more mature way of approach is to accept that not all people are capable of saying goodbye when they feel they don't want to take part in a communication anymore. Not all are capable of processing this emotion and the necessary actions of letting the other party know what is happening. Ghosting is acceptable, normal, healthy for people to do. You can ghost your friends if they are no longer in line with your goals and lifestyle. You can ghost anyone for your peace of mind. 1. Communication on the modern day has gotten on a really different level. It is so fast and the values of people have changed alongside it. Being "seen" is considered somehow an insult. Being "left on read" is a malign. The quick and easy way of responding to messages has gotten most of us in expecting the fastest replies. Gone are the days of snail mail waiting months for a prized message from someone important. 2. It is not surprising that we see new villains the people who ghost. We see them as cruel, insensitive, and why not? They left us hanging with no explanations. Where did we go wrong? Did we become uninteresting? Why can't they just say goodbye? We think a word would satisfy us. But the truth is, ghosting is something that is natural. Just because sending a message is easy doesn't mean it is easy to construct a meaningful one. 3. In a real life communication, not everything we say to someone will be greeted with enthusiasm. Sometimes, they could just gesture a simple nod or a grunt. If they have something to contribute, they will say it and if you are lucky, it would lead to a truly interesting conversation. Now, imagine this in the virtual world. It is cruel to always expect an equally enthusiastic reply from someone. We should accept that truly engaging talks are rare to come by and should be treasured. 4. Expecting someone to keep up conversations with us is putting them in our own projections. We project that we text them, they would reply, we enjoy a fun night talking to each other. The truth is not everyone is capable of doing this. Problems arise, people become sad, people need time and people need to ignore messages. It is then asking too much to expect someone to always and constantly attend to our needs in the virtual plane of communication. 5. Now, let us say that none of the things mentioned were the cause of ignoring or ghosting. What if the other person's interest towards us just fizzled out? Should we blame them for chickening out to say "I am sorry, I believe I am not interested in keeping our communication going." It is not an easy thing to say and definitely something that someone should not be obliged to say. It is our task to accept that we are not always interesting, or that people we talk to don't have fountain sources of replies. We should accept that fizzling out is a characteristic of any conversation. March 21, 11:43:42 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Giving my perspective as the friend who was ghosted before. I can tell you that letting go is something we must do again and again, feeling the sting anew each time. To those who have ghosted someone who once loved or cared for them, understand this: no one lets go in an instant. You might see your departure as a single, swift act, but for the one left behind, the reality is a slow, repetitive unraveling. It happens in the quiet moments when we instinctively reach for our phones to send you a photo of a cute stray cat or a funny stray thought, only to pull our hands back because while your ghost remains, you are no longer there. It happens when we pass a place we once visited or hear an echo of our old conversations. These are constant, tiny heartbreaks—a messy, lingering grief where we are forced to release you piece by piece, moment by moment. Please, do not leave us haunting the spaces you used to fill. Don’t ghost the people who care for you; just give us the truth, no matter how much it hurts. March 20, 10:35:24 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] “Hindi naman yun ang intent ko.” Let me hold your hand when I say this... hindi umiikot sa intent mo ang mundo. Ang dinadala ng tao after the interaction is the impact, kung ano yung naramdaman nila, at hindi kung ano yung gusto mong mangyari. Hindi tayo pare-pareho ng pinanggalingan. Iba-iba tayo ng limits, experiences, at kung ano ang nakaka-trigger sa atin. So yes, pwedeng “normal” lang ‘yan para sa’yo, but that doesn’t automatically make it harmless. And when someone tells you na nasaktan sila or naapektuhan sila, that’s not your cue para mag-defend ng sarili mo like it’s a debate. Hindi ito argument na kailangan mong “ipanalo.” It’s a moment to listen (and understand, not dismiss (kung kaya mo)). Accountability isn’t oppression. Hindi ka inaapi just because someone pointed out na may naging mali sa ginawa mo. Hindi rin ito personal attack. Pero if ang first instinct mo ay i-dismiss, i-minimize, o i-gaslight yung concern, then maybe that says more about YOU than the situation :))) So instead of hiding behind “hindi ko naman intention ‘yun,” you try something new: “Ano bang naging epekto nito sa iba, at ano ang gagawin ko ngayon?” Kasi at the end of the day, hindi intent mo ang naaalala ng tao. Impact mo at impact ng mga ginawa mo :) March 19, 10:49:14 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I just want to share something as it was weighing on my mind for a couple of years now. There's this guy that nireto sakin ng friend ko that time, and syempre I stalked him on everything. I came to know that he is wealthy. At the time, I had a prejudice sa mga mayayaman like na they're masungit and that they wouldn't mingle at all sa mga katulad ko na hindi naman ganun kayaman or anything. Kaya even before we talked, I already have this view of him na baka nga ganun siguro syang tao. So you wouldn't believe how shocked I was when I learned that he was a very caring, kind and responsible person. He was everything that I could've wanted and more. He's very talented. He composes songs and sings it and even produces it all by himself. At first, it was everything to me. I loved his voice, I loved the way he shares reels of the most random of things, I love the way he greets me good morning and how he genuinely asks questions because he want to learn more about me. Looking back, I don't even know how he loved me when my replies are dry af. Then, he wrote me a song. It was the first time in my life that someone ever made me a song. It was so beautiful, that I listen to it now more times than I would care to admit. It was about him wanting to see me after the pandemic is over. Everything was perfect, but that was exactly why I was terrified. I was terrified that all of this were too good to be real and that one day he'd realize that I wasn't good enough. When he said I love you for the first time, I was both ecstatic and terrified. That none of it was real and once he gets to see me and interact with me for real, he'd leave me and I'd be all alone again. This was at the same time that classes started to shift into blended learning. After a few days, I got to meet someone I had a crush on before, and some of my feelings for him started to reappear. I talked with him, and interacted with him because we went to the same school. I talked less and less with the guy na nireto sakin ng friend ko and eventually, I didn't text him anymore. Essentially, I ghosted him. I regret it. I regret it every day. A few months later, I entered a relationship with a new guy. He was sweet, charming, all the works. It didn't work out in the end and he eventually got back with his ex. After a few months, a new school year began and a lot of transferees came. One of them knew the guy na nireto sakin and I found out na he asked about me sa transferee if nandun parin ako sa school and if I was doing well. That crushed me. The thought that I left someone so genuinely kind and broke his heart. What hurt me more was the fact that after all those months, he still cared about me. I told the transferee to tell the guy na nakamove on nako and na I was in a relationship. I wasn't, but I wanted him to move on far from me, so that he wouldn't get hurt, but maybe I was just protecting myself from the consequences of my actions. Maybe I was protecting myself from thinking that I broke that guy's heart. So that I wouldn't be at fault. Diba? Apaka gag*? Di ko man lang sinabi sa kanya ng harapan, or maybe kahit ako nalang nagchat. Through mutual friends pa talaga. Recently, I think about him a lot. I listen to the song he dedicated for me. A lot. I wish him all the happiness in the world. I hope he forgets about me. I hope he h*tes me, because it would hurt me more to know that he isn't mad at me. He took a business major at a major university now. I know because I once asked him what he wanted to take for college. In the off-chance that you see this, I really hope you're happy where you are. I know it's shameless of me to say this after all these years, but, I loved you. March 18, 7:29:52 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Open letter to my former close friend. I don’t regret loving you, I regret how it ended. I wasn’t looking for love when things developed between us. But things really developed quickly. In less than a year, I already fell in love with you. I guess part of it is because it felt like if life can throw us this many curveballs and we are still together, then maybe you are the one. And I guess a part of me always wanted to show you what love truly is. I was there for you during the hardest time of your life, I felt that if the shoe were on the other foot, you’d do the same. I didn’t do that because I wanted a debt of gratitude, but due to us being friends. One random night while we were talking you asked me if I had romantic feelings for you, I never figured that it would be an issue that I fell for you. All I cared about was you. You said you may have commitment or abandonment issues or that you wondered if you’re a bad person. I guess I always tried to reassure that I would never leave you, that I was cocky enough to show you love where you wouldn’t fear commitment. Little did I know that showing more love would bring that fear more. Of course, I tried to play it cool but little did you know that I was so happy. I wanted to make you so happy that I admit there were times I treated you as more than a friend. So when things finally blew up in my face when you realized you don't want to be friends anymore and some boundaries not being maintained, my safety shattered. I didn’t want to fix you. I just wanted to be there for you, so that you can have the best conditions to fix yourself. I knew you were independent and that you can do them on your own. But instead, you were overwhelmed because of me. You said you can’t give me what I deserve or need in terms of romantic love. I was willing to endure but you were more willing to discard. Then you just became a ghost to me anyway. I don’t regret loving you, but I do regret how it ended. I hope you’re doing well. March 16, 12:34:38 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I’ve been that “try hard” friend. The one who honestly believed that how I value people is how they’ll treat me back. Ang naive, I know. Turns out, that mindset is straight up BS. Takot ma ignore, ma undervalue, o maging backup friend lang when it’s convenient for them. Until I became the person I hated. The friend ghoster. I slowly pulled away. Seen messages, no replies. Biglang nawala without explanation. After two weeks, they messaged me saying they missed me. That their days weren’t as fun without me. Naalala nila birthday ko. They greeted me on Christmas and New Year. That’s when it hit me sila pala yung totoong kaibigan. Probably the best ones I’ve had in a long time. And I h*rt them. Hindi dahil may ginawa silang mali, but because I was scared of getting attached again. I realized that in life, you’ll meet a lot of wrong people. And sometimes, the damage they leave turns you into someone you never wanted to be. Pushing people away becomes a coping mechanism self protection disguised as distance. Pero ang sakit, minsan nadadamay yung mga totoong tao. I want to do better. I want to be more present with the people who actually show up. I still want to believe that the right people will stay. [Luzon/Provincial] [OPINION] - Gas Price Hike & 4-Day Work Week Context: US-Israel Conflict/War Law of Supply - less supply, higher prices & more supply, lower prices Resource management: controlled use/distribution of resources for the purpose of, for example, cases of scarcity and survival --- Given the context, we should understand that a lack of supply ng gas/oil will naturally lead to higher prices. If we do not have our own large reserve of it, we will need to buy from other countries that will also up their selling price dahil nagkakaubusan ng gas/oil. To make up for this, tataas din ang prices sa atin to have enough money to import the resource. Wala tayong magagawa kasi kailangan din natin to function - to go to where we need and come back home. In terms of hike sa prices, this is uncontrollable kasi even outside PH, this happens because of wars and conflicts that use up and/or impede the distribution of resources. In relation to this, the 4-day work week is something that I will also oppose because it brings compromises with our education but only under circumstances na wala namang wars/conflicts na nangyayari. When we use transpo vehicles, they use gas/oil. It might be small if we're looking at daily commute lang and personal use sa private cars but all together, they stack up. We can not continue consuming resources at the same rate we normally do because there is scarcity ng resources na 'yon. Distribution/use must be limited if we want to last a bit longer, to survive a bit longer. Hence, limiting movement aids in conserving what little resources we have. Our current situation is not good - not ideal - but it is what we need to do for now given the context of wars/conflicts that is affecting our world. We can only hope for the problems to be resolved as soon as possible so we wouldn't suffer longer. [End of Opinion] March 10, 1:15:34 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I get frustrated when people assume I don’t know how to be happy on my own. I’ve been alone for almost a decade. That’s a long time to sit with yourself. It hasn’t been the most exciting decade of my life, but after that long, you learn how to live with your own company. You get used to it. You make peace with it. So when people talk to me like I’ve never learned how to be alone, it feels off. I already know that life. I’ve been living it. Another thing that gets to me is when people say I need to “work on myself.” I get the idea behind it, but it often sounds like I’m somehow unfinished or not enough as I am. Whatever happened to just being your authentic self? The truth is, everyone is a work in progress for as long as they’re alive. No one wakes up one day and suddenly becomes a fully fixed person. What makes it harder is when the people saying this are also carrying their own damage and loneliness, but they speak like they’ve figured life out. They give advice like they’re standing on higher ground, when really we’re all just trying to get through our own mess. These conversations almost always circle back to the same thing. Love. Finding the right guy. And somehow it always lands on the idea that I must be the problem. Maybe I am. I’ve thought about that more times than I’d like to admit. Sometimes I even wonder if maybe I’m just not meant to be loved. Because when I look back at my past relationships, a pattern shows up. I’m either someone they run to when it’s convenient, or I become a kind of experiment. A place where they try to fix their own issues. And when they’re done, or when they figure themselves out, they leave. Just like that. So before anyone tells me to fix myself, I wish they knew this. I have tried. I’m still trying. Every single day. And somehow, no matter how much effort I put in, I still end up in the same place. Alone. And lonely. March 9, 9:58:16 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I used to admire her quietly. She’s the kind of woman people notice right away; smart, beautiful, the kind of beauty people sometimes mistake for Korean that people assume she is way younger than her actual age. But what made her unforgettable to me was how brave and thoughtful she was. The way she carried herself, the way she cared deeply about things, the way she kept moving forward no matter what, the way she found courage within her when it matters. So I stayed in my place, supporting her the only way I knew how. Tahimik lang, but always there. Cheering for her, believing in her, celebrating even the smallest things she accomplished. And she knew. She appreciated it. Sometimes a simple thank you, sometimes just a smile. But for me, those little things felt bigger than she probably realized. Somewhere along the way, I realized I wasn’t just admiring her anymore. I was rooting for her not just because she was amazing... but because she became someone my heart quietly chose to love. I was already quietly rooting for her with my whole heart. Then one day, it was like a door that suddenly slammed shut from a gust of wind. The conversations that once flowed so easily like a river, suddenly dried up. The space where her name used to appear on my screen became empty. And before I could even understand what was happening, I found myself standing outside a door I could no longer knock on. No goodbye. No last conversation. Just quiet indifference. I won’t lie or pretend, there are still moments I catch myself wondering how she’s doing. Wishing she’s okay. Wishing life is being kind to her the way she deserves. But even with that silence, I don’t regret anything. And if my role in her story was simply to genuinely admire her, believe in her, and love her quietly from afar... then I’m still grateful that, even for a while, my world got to have someone like her in it. -YearnerFinalBoss March 7, 9:03:32 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I am the type of person who genuinely loved supporting someone I admired. I was always there—cheering for her, encouraging her, and being proud of every little thing she achieved. But one thing I’m sure of—I don’t regret supporting her. Everything I did came from a genuine place. Because I love her. And given the chance I'll do it again. [Luzon/Provincial] So, I officially lost my cool today (6 Mar 26) at the Forestry waiting shed. ​If you’ve ever queued for a Forestry jeep after 4PM, you know the stakes. The time in between jeeps is erratic, the line is growing by the second, and the humidity (especially now na summer na) is testing your last nerve. My students and I had been waiting forever, joking about how we’d finally snap if someone dared to cut the line. Little did I know, I was foreshadowing my own villain origin story. ​ ​The jeep finally rolls up, but Manong stops it right in the middle of the queue—an irritating error to me (you'll know why). Well, to my absolute horror, two students (judging by the youthful audacity) who were at the back of the queue didn’t even glance at the line. They just bolted for the front passenger seat. This immediately triggered a "Hunger Games" or a "Zombie Apocalypse" response. Suddenly, everyone (except my students, who stayed disciplined) tried to abandoned the queue and seemingly about to swarm the jeep like it was the last helicopter out of a disaster zone. ​ ​I was so livid that my professional filter just... EVAPORATED. Before I could stop myself, I yelled out to the crowd: ​"Mga b*b* ba kayo?!" ​It sorta worked. Most people froze, suddenly remembering they were supposed to be functioning members of a civil society. But those two students in the front seat? They just sat there, laughing like they hadn't just spit on the concept of basic decency. I was shooting d*ggers at them, but apparently, they’re immune to public shame. ​It’s honestly frustrating. We like to talk about "nation-building" and "serving the people," but we can’t even handle the intellectual complexity of a single-file line. If our scholars (ng bayan, para sa bayan?) and workers are this quick to ignore proper systems the moment things get slightly inconvenient, no wonder this country is in a constant state of chaos. Disrespect for the queue is just a gateway dr*g to larger forms of corruption. ​ ​So, was I wrong? Should I have just "embraced the chaos" and let the law of the jungle take over, since forestry naman yun? Or is it okay to occasionally remind people that being a "scholar" includes having the common sense to wait your turn? ​Because if "Mga b*b* ba kayo?!" is the wrong response to a total collapse of discipline, I don't think I want to be right anymore. P.S. Sarap sampigahin nung dalawa talaga. One biologically boy and one biologically girl (not sure if anong sogie nila). Basta sure akong sing GANDA nila yung ugali nila. Si Manong driver naman walampake. Sana turuan mo din mga batang "pag-asa" sana ng bayan. Pera pera na lang ba, Tay? OR ganun ba talaga sa upper campus ngayon at wala na talagang queueing culture? I'd really like to know. (image is used as a location reference only, di po yan yung jeep namin kanina) March 6, 10:09:08 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] "Unresolved feelings" Lately napapanaginipan ko siya. This week, sunod-sunod na gabi ko na siyang napapanaginipan. Minsan nagcha-chat daw siya sa panaginip ko, minsan tumatawag, tapos minsan naman nakikita ko lang siyang dumadaan. Nakikita ko siya, pero hindi kami nag-uusap. Parang nandiyan lang siya, pero wala talagang interaction. Ang nakakatawa pa, bago nagsimula lahat ng panaginip na ’to, sure na sure na ako sa sarili ko na naka-move on na ako. Proud pa nga akong sinabi sa mga kaibigan ko na okay na ako. Tapos biglang ganito. Parang biglang bumalik lahat. Mapapaisip ka tuloy kung pinaglalaruan ka ba ng utak mo. Nung sinearch ko online kung ano ibig sabihin kapag paulit-ulit mong napapanaginipan ang isang tao, ang tumatak talaga sa akin ay unresolved feelings. Siguro nga totoo. Kasi kung tutuusin, wala naman talagang closure. Isang araw bigla na lang siyang nawala. Parang naglaho lang sa buhay ko. Parang ghost lang na biglang nag-disappear. Ilang gabi ko rin siyang iniyakan noon. Umabot pa sa point na nag-beg ako na ayusin namin, na mag-usap man lang kami nang maayos. Pero wala. Lahat nangyari lang online—pati yung pagkawala niya. Ang hirap tanggapin kapag ganun, kasi ang daming tanong na naiwan. Bakit hindi man lang niya sinubukang ayusin? Bakit hindi man lang ako kinausap nang maayos, kahit isang beses, kahit personal? Aminado naman ako na naging dependent ako noon. Pero sana kahit konting respeto man lang sa kung anong meron kami. Sana sinabi na lang niya nang diretso kung ano talaga. Ang hirap kasi kapag bigla ka na lang iiwan sa gitna ng lahat, tapos ikaw na lang mag-isa ang maghahanap ng sagot. Ngayon, parang kailangan ko na lang tanggapin na ako na mismo ang gagawa ng closure para sa sarili ko. Kasi kung hihintayin ko pa siya, baka hindi na talaga ako makausad. Kaya napangako ko na rin sa sarili ko na siguro hindi na muna ako magjojowa hanggang 30. Ang dami ko pang gustong gawin para sa sarili ko, at unfair din naman kung magko-commit ako sa iba habang hindi pa ako totally okay. Buti na lang may mga nababasa akong stories na kahit nasa 30s na, may mga nagkakaroon pa rin ng talking stage o nakakahanap ng tao para sa kanila. Nakaka-comfort kahit papaano. Siguro ang pinakamahirap lang ngayon ay yung loneliness. Iba na rin kasi kapag tumatanda ka—hindi na tulad dati na laging available ang mga kaibigan mo. May kanya-kanya nang buhay. May mga araw talaga na pakiramdam mo mag-isa ka lang sa mundo. Minsan naiisip ko rin: baka tumanda na lang talaga akong mag-isa. Pero sana kung mangyari man ’yun, masaya ako. Sana naabot ko pa rin lahat ng pangarap ko. Kaya ko lang naman ’to shinare kasi matagal na siyang umiikot sa isip ko. At kung sakaling wala talagang darating para sa akin, siguro okay lang din. Ako na lang muna para sa sarili ko. Deserve ko rin naman mahalin. Kahit sa ngayon, manggagaling muna sa akin. Sana mapanindigan ko. 🤍 March 6, 2:40:18 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Dati di naman ako madalas magkape. Once a day sometimes just thrice a week but lately, napapadalas. Why is it in such a time na I would have you cross my mind, doon ako napapadalas magkape? I was yet unaware hanggang sa narealize ko, kanina lang, kaya siguro ako nagkakape para maging excuse ito pag nakaramdam ako ng malakas na kabog sa dibdib ko. Iisipin ko "ah, naparami nanaman siguro yung pagkape ko." Na kung minsan pag bibili ako ng kape yung pinakamatapang yung bibilhin ko. Siguro nakundisyon ko na yung utak ko na kailangan kong uminom ng kapeng malakas para mapagkamalan ko yung pagsakit ng dibdib ko as something most people feel if they've had too much coffee. Palpitation. Mas madali kasing tanggapin na mabilis ang tibok ng puso ko dahil sa caffeine, kaysa aminin na gumagaralgal ito dahil sayo. [Luzon/Provincial] Daming gbf/bbf posting na may gf/bf haha Wala namang masama sa pagkakaroon ng kaibigan, male-female friendship is okay (or vice-versa) as long as each party involved know their boundaries. Hindi lang dapat yung magjowa yung nag-aadjust or may set boundaries, pati din dapat yung gbf/bbf haha. To the one in a relationship na may GBF/BBF: You should be honest with your partner and have clear boundaries na napagkasunduan niyong dalawa. Ask your partner how comfortable they are sa friendship niyo ng gbf/bbf mo, and compromise what both of you agreed on. Please, always prioritize or choose your partner. If your partner is uncomfortable with your gbf/bbf, you have to do something about it pero not to the point i-cut off unless there are other reasons to do so. Sabihin mo sa kaibigan mo na hindi comfortable jowa mo sa friendship niyong dalawa, and maybe try mo include jowa mo, magsama kayong tatlo para makapag-bond kayo. Be the bridge sa partner and sa gbf/bbf mo na magkasundo sila, that's a way for your partner to trust your friend or trust the both of you. If your gbf/bbf doesn't agree with what you proposed and prefers na ikaw lang palagi kasama or ayaw niyang i-acknowledge jowa mo, that's one reason why your partner might not trust your gbf/bbf or even you kasi if you allow this idea, you're allowing your friend to disrespect your partner. Sayo mismo dapat manggaling na partner mo yun, it is your responsibility to protect your bf/gf and call out your friend if he/she is disrespecting or did something na your bf/gf feels uncomfortable. You should have clear boundaries sa gbf/bbf mo kung hanggang saan lang sila sa buhay mo and kung ano lang yung p'wede niyong magawa and hindi dapat gawin na might violate your partner's trust, this does not only include cheating but things that your partner is uncomfortable with. A friend should understand and be happy about their friend's milestones especially sa romantic relationship, he/she should be the biggest supporter or an ally sa relationship niyo ng jowa niyo, hindi yung nagiging point of conflict. To the GBF/BBF: You have to acknowledge their partner's existence, understand na your friend should prioritize their partner. Huwag feeling entitled na kung ano nakasanayan niyo na kayo lang palagi, ikaw na palagi; dumating lang yung jowa, nagkaroon ng changes. Check your label sissy/bro, kaibigan ka lang, hindi ikaw jowa. You might say na walang malisya yung friendship niyo and hindi ka naman interested sa friend mo na purely platonic lang naman, you have to understand na the 'intent' is not the same as 'impact'. Kahit sabihin mong 'walang malisya' sa side mo or kayong dalawa, kung ang actions niyo ay nag-ca-cause ng gulo sa relationship nila, ikaw rin ang kailangang mag-adjust kahit na sabihin mong you do not have a responsibility sa relationship nila kasi relationship nila yun, which is totoo naman. If kaibigan mo talaga kaibigan mo, you will do something to help them have a harmonious relationship. Hindi porke 'nauna' ka ay may 'legacy rights' ka na sa oras at atensyon ng friend mo. One thing you can do is—kung paano mo tinatrato yung friend mo, you should treat their partner with the same level of respect and effort. Acknowledge that the partner isn't just an 'addition' to your bubble; they are now the center of your friend’s world. Hindi yung kapag magkakasama kayo, yung friend mo lang ang kinakausap mo at parang 'hangin' lang yung partner sa tabi niya. If you bring a gift or food for your friend, bring some for the partner too. If you’re planning a hangout, include their partner too, huwag lang puro si friend mo. Don’t make the partner feel like an afterthought or a 'sabit'. Hindi enough ang pagtanong lang sa friend mo kung okay lang ba sa jowa nila na magkasama kayo, you have make an action too about it. To the girlfriend/boyfriend: You have to acknowledge too that your partner's friend is also important to your partner, it's up to your partner how they will set boundaries with them. It's okay to speak up, valid ang nararamdaman mo kung naiilang ka sa dynamics nila. Having boundaries doesn't make you "toxic" or "controlling"—it means you value the sanctity of your relationship. Huwag mong kimkimin ang selos hanggang sa sumabog ka. Communicate with your partner kung ano yung uncomfortable sayo, be clear on what you want and need pero never ask your partner to cut off their friend, that is unfair for your partner and to the friend, yung jowa mo dapat ang magde-decide kung necessary bang i-cut off friend niya, case-to-case basis naman ang pag-cut off, if sobrang malala na nag-overstep si gbf/bbf sa boundaries set, that's a valid example for your partner to do it kasi na-violate yung boundaries ng partner mo as an individual. Be open sa friendship, give them a chance, open your doors for a group hangout. Kung makita mong rerespetuhin ka nila at itratrato ka nang maayos at they make you feel included, mas madaling magtiwala. Trust your partner but never dismiss what you feel to accommodate your partner's and their friend's comfort, huwag kang people pleaser, you have to communicate clearly with your partner. At the end of the day, partner mo ang may commitment sa'yo, hindi yung kaibigan. If your partner is doing their best to prioritize you and set those boundaries and firm sa boundaries nila sa friend nila, appreciate that effort. You deserve to feel like the priority. If the "friendship" is constantly being used as an excuse to disregard your feelings, then it’s not just a friendship problem—it’s a relationship problem. To everyone: Friendship is a choice, but a committed relationship is a covenant. After all, this is a 3-way street, lahat kailangang mag-adjust. Magulong sitwasyon yung ganitong bagay kasi lumaki tayo sa stigma naka-instill na sa atin na kapag ang lalaki at babae ay magkasama, laging may 'posibleng malisya' na hindi naman dapat. Dahil dito, minsan ang partner ay nagiging overly defensive, at ang gbf/bbf naman ay nagiging overly rebellious sa boundaries. Platonic friendship can exist without hidden agenda. Pwedeng maging magkaibigan lang ang magkaibang kasarian nang walang malisya. Ang problema, ginagawa nating 'excuse' yung stigma para hindi mag-set ng boundaries, o kaya naman ay ginagamit nating 'weapon' yung stigma para hindi pagkatiwalaan o ma-uncomfortable sa friendship nila ng babaeng/lalaking kaibigan ng partner natin. March 5, 10:19:50 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] On Loving Unconditionally and Smiling Genuinely I was twenty, drowning in the quiet chaos of my worst university semester, when we met. It was a season when I had stopped chasing the possibility of romantic love, having had zero experience with it anyway. I was carefree yet failing, floating through disappointed stares and existing at my absolute lowest. He met me there, in that wreckage—and perhaps, in his own way, I met him at his lowest, too. By the end of the semester, we were constantly talking. Before we left for our respective provinces, we agreed to have dinner—just the two of us—a small, private anchor before the distance of the break. By the end of the year, he asked if he could start courting me. I said yes, and we stepped into January feeling like a fresh beginning—no longer just two people talking, but two people officially finding their way to each other. I remember the chill of that first week in . We were back at the university, navigating paperwork and the nervous energy of new affection. After a trip to an amusement park—a short escape from our looming responsibilities—the shift happened. I don’t remember the rides or the noise, but I remember the choice. I was the one who reached out first. I was the one who initiated the holding of hands, letting the warmth of his palm confirm what my heart was just beginning to admit. On January 19th, I told him, "Yes," and we became official. He asked for permission to kiss me, a gesture so tender it set the tone for the years to follow. He became my MOON, the light that didn’t eliminate the dark but guided me through it. In his eyes, I had no flaws. He was my best friend, my protector, and the person I believed would carry me through anything. The Shadow and the Light But love, even unconditional love, has its darkness. There were days of silence where messages went unreturned, moments where I felt “too much,” and nights where someone cried their heart out while the other stayed quiet. It wasn’t perfect, but it held value. In our first year, I remember seeing a post he made. He said he would be a little happier if someone gifted him the book series A Song of Ice and Fire and Fire and Blood by George R. R. Martin. I had no money then, only meager savings, but I spent weeks hunting for a pre-loved set I could afford. When I finally found one, it felt like more than a gift; it was a testament. To me, he deserved everything, and I wanted to be the one to provide the happiness he was looking for. The Distance and the Depletion Then the world slowed down. The pandemic forced us into a long-distance struggle, testing us in environments we weren’t prepared for. When the lockdowns lifted, I convinced my parents to let me move back near the university just to be near him. But life remained complicated; he chose not to see me most days because his sister was pregnant at the time. I completely understood, yet I still found myself begging for a presence I thought I had earned by moving closer. It felt like I was the only one trying to close the distance. It took a freak accident—a needle puncturing my finger while I was sewing and a subsequent surgery—for the universe to force us into the same room for longer than mere hours. For a while, the “softness” returned. At his sister’s suggestion, he stayed with me. We lived together, we studied together, and I finally began to pass the subjects that had once defeated me. My family fully embraced him, opening the doors of our home and our lives to him without reservation. We grew up within that shared space. We were twenty when we started; we were nearly thirty when we stopped. We stood by each other through final defenses and shared the triumph of a delayed graduation. But even with that success, I ended things before the date of our graduation ceremony arrived. The Hard Choice of Letting Go We broke up for the first time in , only to gravitate back together almost a year later. But by [REDACTED_NAME] , I realized that loving someone unconditionally can also mean becoming completely spent. It felt like I’d been carrying more than I could handle—emotionally, mentally, even silently. I tried to make things work, hoping the love I had would be enough to carry us through. But the truth is, I was tired. Our relationship was just dragging, and we weren’t growing as individuals anymore. I was staying not because I felt full, but because I was afraid of what breaking away would do—to him, to us, to everything we’d built. Ending things again felt cruel. As the decision took hold, I began to punish myself relentlessly; it felt as though being the villain in our story was sharply carved into my very being. I questioned how I could be the source of such wreckage for the person I loved most in the world, not once, but twice. I felt beyond redemption. Yet, looking back from , I still see the necessity of the pain. We had to grow. We couldn’t just settle into an environment that felt “comfortable” because we should be the ones who make the environment we stay in safe and comfortable for both of us—and we were not capable of doing exactly that in that moment. The Smile in the Frame Today, I look at the few photos of us I have left. I study my face—that specific, radiant smile. It was a genuine smile, the kind that only exists when you believe your heart is fully held. My smile today doesn’t look like that. I haven’t reached acceptance yet. There is still enough hope to make me want to stay, but enough pain to make me realize I must step back for his sake as much as my own. I have lost him, and in the process, I fear I have lost that version of my smile, too. I loved him without conditions at the beginning, and I am letting him go now with the hardest condition of all: the need for us both to finally, truly, grow up. And if ever God brings us back together, I hope we return as whole people—capable of honoring the weight, seeing the truth, and protecting the sanctity of love exactly the way it should be. March 5, 6:26:47 AM +08

Fast forward to February we are talking about this coming activity and I look at her eyes and it happened — I fell in love. Maybe it is her being amazing at almost everything because she works hard, keeps at it until she gets it right. I already knew my feelings are not reciprocated but I can tell there is something there. I confessed. I told her I like her, but I avoided using the word love or that I fell for her because she could withdraw from me even as friends. I wanted to know. I wanted answers. I was almost praying — I am atheist — she feels the same and half praying there could be something. I do not know anymore. Maybe it is my fault for falling but god knows every man in this world will fall for her if they know her. At lalong hindi ‘yan dapat sinasabi nang walang pahintulot ng taong involved. Hindi porket galing kami sa mass recruitment, required na kaming maging trophy members para lang magmukhang bongga ang event numbers mo. Tao kami. May pinagdadaanan. May hangganan. [Luzon/Provincial] random dump for ff '26 food favorites ! 1. pueblo's ilocos empanada - 9.5/10 - ang sarap talaga ! sa vega, sa elbi community market, and sa booth nila ako bumili and lagi talagang fresh and warm :> would recommend the papaya varianteu 2. kalugar's shawarma wrap - 9/10 - might say the best shawarma in ff '26?! ang fresh ng ingredients and hindi siya kasing-oily ng other stalls that i've tried. super dami pang servings and ang sarap ng sauces ! worth the money yey ^__^ 3. kochi matcha and hojicha's matcha latté - 9.5/10 - may nakatalo na sa favorite matcha ko in elbi (bitrersweet crumbs) ! i love how grassy it is and hindi siya powdery unlike the matcha lattés available in the market. may pagka-fishy taste siya na weirdly nagustuhan ko ehe. hoping they'll have a physical store soon :)) + their hojicha latté is even better ! nutty, toasty, and super earthy ! may slight sweetness din from the oatmilk so balanced talaga 'yung flavors :3 4. kapeng barako ng upc - 7.5 /10 - i love na hindi siya similar sa tapang ng other coffees sa market ! ang smooth niya lang din inumin + relatively affordable so value for money talaga ! hoping na maging regular fra siya hehe (i.e. pwedeng ibenta in containers) because i feel like it's perfect for all nighters ! and lastly... 5. trimoo's yogurt - 7/10 - i love na may customizable yogurt stall sa ff '26! masarap 'yung yogurt-based na option, okay ang syrups and sauces, and ang fresh ng fruits ! i remembered yowell since super similar sila ng flavor profile :)) February 23, 3:50:53 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Mabigat talaga para sa’kin ito, but I guess this is where our story changes. Almost one year din ‘yon. Hindi man sobrang tagal sa iba, pero for me, ang daming nangyari sa loob ng panahong ‘yon. You were my first boyfriend. Sa’yo ko unang naranasan lahat, first monthsary, first real arguments, first “I love you,” first time na may taong siguradong-sigurado sa’kin. I still remember how you made me feel so safe. So chosen. You loved me in a way that made me stop looking for anyone else. Parang ikaw na talaga. You were everything I asked God for — kind, patient, consistent, sure about me. And because of that, mas lalo kitang minahal. But habang tumatagal, mas naging malinaw din sa’kin yung reality natin. We both know how firm we are with our faith. I respect you for standing strong in yours, and you respected me for mine. Pero doon din tayo nagkaroon ng pinakamalaking problema. Hindi pwede sa religion namin na makipagrelasyon sa ibang paniniwala. At kahit anong pilit nating maging hopeful, alam natin sa loob natin na may limitasyon. Masakit kasi walang third party. Walang nagloko. Walang kulang sa pagmamahal. In fact, we loved each other the right way. But sometimes, love isn’t the only thing that matters. There were nights I kept asking myself, “What if kaya?” What if there was no restriction? What if we were allowed? I know for sure I would choose to stay. I would choose you without hesitation. Pero paano tayo magpapatuloy kung kailangan tayong magtago? If we can’t freely tell the world about us? I wanted to be proud of you. I wanted to introduce you without fear. I wanted a love that didn’t need to be hidden. And that’s why this hurts so much. Because I’m not leaving you because I stopped loving you. I’m letting go because I love my faith, and I know you love yours too. And neither of us should have to lose ourselves just to keep each other. Mahal na mahal kita. That will always be true. Siguro hanggang dito muna ang story natin. And I’ll keep praying that God takes care of you, guides you, and blesses you. And if one day, in His perfect time, our paths cross again — when there are no more barriers, maybe we’ll get the ending we once hoped for. But for now, I’ll treasure what we had. Because even if it hurts, it was real. And you will always be my first love. February 23, 10:33:17 AM +08

Paumanhin sa mga kulang na jeepney stops dito. Kinailangan kong sakyan ng mahigit isang taon ang mga ikot jeep dito para lang makumpirma na tama ang mga karagdagang stops ni [Luzon/Provincial], kung saan ang tamang babaan at sakayan. Dibale, noong nakita ko 'yung mockup mo, nabuhayan ako ng loob. Two years in the making, hindi ko naisip na may gagawa din pala ng mockup na talagang printed at laminated. Nakakatuwa at ginawa mo siya! Anyway, explain ko bakit ganyan 'yung project namin sa CERP 101: Ang "tactical urbanism" ay isang grassroots planning initiative na puwedeng i-adopt ng isang community. Nakita naman natin na kulang ang navigation signages dito sa campus, maging ang mga waiting shed hindi consistent. May mga jeepney stops na walang waiting shed: kulang sa bubong para sa ulan, bulletin board para sa mga anunsyo at lalagyan ng mapa, at upuan man lang. (For the Metro Manila commuting nerds: dito sa pitch namin, binase namin ang layout sa environmental design ng LRT–1.) Ang ideyang ito bilang inisyatibo ay dapat ipapadaan sa tamang channels kung nais nating mabuo ito sa labas ng FW. Mainam na ma-recognize muna ito ng mga karampatang opisina upang maipagtibay ang paggamit nito bilang opisyal na navigation amenities sa campus. Maaaring makatulong ang paglapit sa mga karampatang opisina sa [Luzon/Provincial], kagaya ng nasabi ni OP isang post. Baka puwede din natin ito ipadala sa mga kaguruan na nagtuturo ng Planning dito sa ating campus. PS. If I see na more than three of us are willing to craft this as a proposal, sagot ko na 'yung mapa hahaha! If at least six tayo... why not, let's make a mapping org (parang UPRI YouthMappers) na OSA registered, tapos gawin natin itong pilot project? The more, the merrier, pero oo libre lang mangarap for now. ;) —cosma(p)apolitan (hume 125 enjoyer) February 22, 8:15:00 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] I honestly didn't think that it's possible to gather up my confidence as an acne girlie, but seeing this one girl in my class literally changed my whole life and I hope she sees this message. For context, I've been admiring this girl in my class nung first day pa lang and I was too shy to even befriend her. First of all she styles her clothes sooo good, she always wear brown, wears make-up, and the only thing we have in common is acne. I always admired how she looks, never seen her unconfident— part of me thought it was because of wearing make-up but I was so wrong! I never tried to style my clothes or even wear make-up so my mindset was like that and I was never proven so wrong my whole life. I think a month has passed this 2nd sem and seeing her so consistent styling clothes and wearing make-up made me honestly give up on looking good with acne because I don't have the m-ntal and physical capacity to even just wake-up early and think of what I'm gonna wear and apply make-up. That mindset changed one day because this week, this girl showed up to class not wearing make-up and just some casual clothes and she still looks so goddamn good!!! I'm not saying that not wearing make-up = ugly to other people but that's what I thought for myself. She was also still confident speaking and not lowering her composure (like I do 😢) and it made me realize I'm not ugly because of my acne, I just need that confidence like she has. I know how hard it is to have acne and I think it didn't take her just a few hours to get that confidence, she worked hard to build that and I feel so sad that I didn't start building my own confidence sooner. If you're seeing this, I just want to let you know that I'm so so thankful that you made me realize everything I needed to reflect on about myself. I hope you continue being confident without thinking what others think and also for all acne girlies out there! soon I hope I can apply it to myself also.. - iskang acne girly February 21, 10:09:18 PM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] Hello!! I am the student creator of the transit diagram recently featured on [Luzon/Provincial]. Initially, passion project lang talaga siya, pero I realized that transit information around the campus would be a great help to first-timers and visitors. Tanda ko as a freshie not knowing how to get around, and I realized that clear navigation information around campus, especially in designated jeepney stops would better help the commuting experience. Lalo na marami nagtatanong how to get to certain places around campus, naming the designated jeepney stops would be better for navigation as they would become landmarks themselves. Unfortunately, kahit na yung jeepney talaga yung pinaka-nagmamakeup ng transport system natin, wala naman tayong sufficient navigational tools para malaman kung paano ito gamitin wisely, or how to get to where we want to go, and that’s why I designed these diagrams. I want to turn these diagrams into permanent, weather-proof fixtures across the campus. I was even planning to expand these, as I already have maps ready for the inner portion of our campus, but as an individual student, I have hit the limit of my personal resources for production and maintenance. I am able to provide the resources for printing and I have the graphics art of the maps as I made them myself. I also have paper and laminating film, but I have no laminating machine and only used an iron kaya nabasa rin yung ibang mga maps sa Kaliwa (KL) line na naexpose sa rain recently. I am looking for any kind of support to help better this initiative. May mga student resources ba that offer free use of laminating machine? Or if merong other way to make the diagrams more permanent. Any support would be appreciated. I have decided to keep my identity anonymous, and those interested or have suggestions can leave a comment on this post, thank you! February 20, 7:06:47 AM +08

[Luzon/Provincial] There is a warmth in [Luzon/Provincial] that is hard to explain, but you feel it instantly. You feel so safe here. It is a rare and beautiful thing that we will carry with us forever. But life can also be rigid. It is not always soft. It is not always easy. And because life is hard, none of us should have to fight it alone. You carry so much weight on your shoulders, but you do not ask for help. You keep it inside. But if life was easy, there would be no need for teachers. There would be no need for mentors or even helpers. We have them because we are not meant to survive this on our own. Sometimes, there are people standing right next to you who want to help. They are ready. But because you don't ask, they don't know. That is what hurts the most. We miss the chance to lift each other up just because of silence. You are special. You deserve to be graced. You deserve kindness and support. Do not be afraid to let people in. [Luzon/Provincial] The Marginalized Friend Ansakit pala noh, yung lagi kalang second thought na friend. Tanggap ko naman actually, na I don't fit as well with the group, but even then it just h*rts. You see your friends walking away from you to go somewhere, and you don't even get an invite. When things get rough between you and someone from the said group, you don't even get the chance to explain your side. It's just awkward silence; ang motto nila pagdating sayo "time heals", but at the end of the day, you'd still be the first one to reach out. Or maybe, I'm misinterpreting everything. Maybe I'm just too d*mb that I thought I belonged in the first place. Or maybe I just care too much and love too much? I think about them many times, and perhaps, I'm too invested into what we have as friends. But damn, can I even blame myself? I've always felt like a commodity -- a convenience, someone easy to talk with, not much drama, agreeable, generous, and what not. I'm one of those, you can just leave him alone, resilient friend, and you can always come back to him; and as much as I agree with that, I also want to feel loved and looked after at. I started to believe that I am no longer seen as such, but I think I still am. Nature doesn't reward the good, the kind -- these are constructs after all. But damn, at least reassure me that I belong, that I may still reach out. Am I too kind? Am I too old? Am I too agreeable; should I disagree more? Why does it feel like cruel and evil people have better friendships than I do? This s-cks man. -02-11 19:46:33

[Luzon/Provincial] In reply to [Luzon/Provincial]. While the concern about microplastics and waste is valid, placing the primary responsibility on small student retailers oversimplifies the issue. Student organizations during events like FebFair operate within real constraints of affordability, availability, and environmental conditions such as muddy grounds and rain. Shoe covers address a practical need for mobility and safety, not mere convenience. I personally chose to buy shoe covers because I only have one pair of shoes, and if those get muddy, I would need to spend at least ₱400 for professional cleaning—an expense that is not feasible for many students. In this context, shoe covers are a preventive and cost-effective option. Environmental responsibility is shared, not unilateral. If disposable products are being sold, accountability must also extend to event organizers who manage waste systems, consumers who are responsible for proper disposal, and institutions that are expected to provide accessible sustainable alternatives and supporting infrastructure. Expecting student sellers to resolve systemic waste issues without institutional backing shifts responsibility downward and distracts from addressing the root causes. When discussing wasted resources, it is also important to consider trade-offs. Professional shoe cleaning involves water, detergents, electricity, transportation, and packaging, all of which carry environmental costs. Preventive measures, even if temporary, can reduce repeated resource use over time, particularly for students who do not have the privilege of multiple footwear options. Ecological literacy should therefore push for better systems—such as proper waste management, collection mechanisms, and clearer sustainability guidelines from organizers—rather than singling out student initiatives responding to immediate and real conditions. Sustainability works best when solutions are collective, practical, and supported at all levels. True ecological literacy recognizes that sustainability must be accessible, practical, and inclusive. Critiquing student sellers and buyers without addressing structural limitations risks turning environmentalism into a privilege—something only those with financial flexibility can afford. -02-09 09:24:35

I didn't contact him once after splitting but at the start of this sem, he messaged me about an injury he got and I couldn't help it. I helped treated his wounds because I honestly still care about him. But I didn't plan on going back. He opened up about how he missed me so much and that he spent days being miserable and crying here and there. I didn't have that experience during our split because I had finally experienced only having to think of myself for myself and it made me realize I wasn't insecure, I didn't have anx**ty, and I slept well. I wish he'd just apologize, not even like how I did to him but just acknowledge what he did h*rt me. [Luzon/Provincial] Hi... I don’t really know how to start this, but I want to take this chance to finally confess what I’ve been feeling. This isn’t the usual kind of confession, because the person I like isn’t just anyone—he was my professor. Yes, my professor. He was new at [Luzon/Provincial] back then, coming from a different home unit, and he started a year before I graduated. I first had him as my professor when he was still new. He was incredibly kind, and honestly, I didn’t even see his full face at the time because he always wore a face mask. Still, there was something about his presence that stood out. He had this gentleness that affected me in a way I couldn’t explain. His voice was so calming whenever he spoke, and even something as simple as his perfectly wavy hair made him memorable. I wasn’t sure what I was feeling whether it was just a crush or pure inspiration but I knew it was something I wanted to keep. The following semester, he became my professor again, this time in around three of my subjects. I would sometimes fall asleep during his last class of the day, partly because my schedule was exhausting, but also because his voice was so calming. During that time, I was often absent due to health issues. Despite that, he never made me feel uncomfortable about it. He helped me catch up, allowed me to take missed requirements, and showed so much consideration. He never questioned my absences—he simply understood. There was even one activity I completely missed, and he personally guided me so I could still complete it. There is this one time where I was shocked that he noticed that I was in that room the entire day and told me that “Kaya ka pala lagi inaantok sa class ko buong araw ka pala dito.” That made me feel noticed. There was also a conference we both attended. Maybe because I was excited, I greeted him with an energy I had never shown before. Throughout the conference, I would catch small glimpses of him every day, exchange brief conversations, and quietly appreciate those moments. In my last semester, I thought I would never see him again. Then one day, as I was entering my classroom, I saw him coming out of his office. We passed each other in silence, and I thought that would be the last time. But during my defense, I was shocked to see him as one of my panelists. I gave my best that day maybe because I wanted to impress him. The entire time, I could barely look at him while I was speaking. It felt like the longest moment of my life. And that was the last time I saw him. You may never read this, and maybe this confession will always remain unspoken. But I want to thank you for being my inspiration in ways you’ll never know. Every time I saw you, I couldn’t help but smile. I know your messages to me were always purely academic, and I understood that. Still, I couldn’t help feeling butterflies whenever you reached out, even if it was just to ask something related to class. I hope that someday, I get to see you again and maybe, if I ever find the courage, I’ll finally thank you in person. iska202xxx -02-07 12:14:21
