[Luzon/Provincial] Valentine's week will be here soon; hence I made a short story in the spirit of it. I am not a writer, nor have I written anything similar to this recently, so I sincerely apologize for any errors. Please enjoy One day, Varka and I were walking along the roads of Mondstadt, when he suddenly knelt down on one knee and proposed to me. "Traveler, I want to be yours for the rest of my life. Will you take my hand in marriage?" he spoke. Of course, since it's Varka, I instantly accepted his proposal. "Yes, my dearest Varka. I will be yours, and you will be mine 'til death do us part. I love you so much!" I replied. And then, we walked hand-in-hand around Mondstadt, announcing and celebrating our engagement. As time passed on, we made arrangements and preparations for our wedding day. We checked out flower shops, saved up millions of mora and fates, and picked out our wedding locations together. One day, while we were rewriting the limits of love underneath the tree in Windrise, the skies became overcast with red clouds. And before we knew it, the Heavenly Principles came down from the heavens. "We have been awakened and notified of your wrongdoings, Descender. You have committed the sin of forming a romantic bond with a Teyvatian human, despite of your alien origins. Hence, you shall be punished greatly for it. Please behave accordingly and do not repeat this offense. This is the first warning" they echoed from the sky. Before I could take out my sword, they hold out their arm in the direction of my Varka. As soon as they moved their hand, I witnessed every being of Varka morphed into something no longer human. I looked away for a moment, as I could not bear to see his disfigurement. I quickly scanned the area, in hopes that anyone else could be witnessing this, but as it turns out, everything aside from me, the Heavenly Principles, and Varka were frozen in time. After what felt like a long time, the Heavenly Principles disappeared, and the skies turned back to normal. Once my gaze returned to the love of my life, I could no longer recognize the sight before me. The form that was supposed to be my fiancé opened its mouth: "Ma-a-a-a" it bleated. I could not believe the cruel punishment the heavens bestowed upon me. Varka, the man who proposed to me weeks ago, reduced to a mere mammal. Despite this, I attempted to speak to it. "Varka, are you okay? It's me, Traveler." I choked out as I held back my tears. "Ma-a-a" it spoke before walking off to a distance. I tried to give chase, but as soon as it heard me running, it also started to run. Then, I made a horrifying realization: it does not recognize me at all, nor does it have the rationality to remember the time we spent together. It took a long while to catch up to my "Varka", who only stopped because it fell into the river near the Mondstadt bridge. In that moment, I just broke down. Because then, I knew, My goat is washed. -02-06 20:35:43

[Luzon/Provincial] I did not expect graduate school at [Luzon/Provincial] to feel this heavy. I came in with quiet hope. Not confidence, just the belief that hard work would be enough. I told myself I belonged here because I earned the slot, because I loved the field, because I was willing to stay up late and read one more paper. Then the grades came. Not failures, just low. Numbers that carried weight. Mediocre. That word hurt more than I thought it would. Each semester ended the same way, not with shock, just a steady disappointment. I checked my grades alone. No noise. Just me and the screen. I stared longer than needed, as if waiting might change something. It never did. I closed the tab and felt small in a place that once felt exciting. The classes were hard, but the harder part was watching myself grow quiet. I stopped raising my hand. I stopped asking questions unless I was sure they were safe. Others spoke with ease about papers I struggled through. I did the work. I submitted on time. Still, the feedback stayed flat. Not harsh, not kind. Just enough to remind me I was average here. I began to doubt myself. Maybe I am not smart enough. Maybe I was wrong to be here. Those thoughts came late at night, when there was nothing to distract me. From the outside, everything looked fine. I was still enrolled. Still passing. People said I was doing okay. I smiled, because explaining felt harder than staying silent. There were days I wanted to quit, not because I h*ted the work, but because I was tired of feeling behind. But I stayed. Not out of strength, just stubbornness. I kept showing up. I learned that staying does not always look brave. Sometimes it is quiet, dull, and lonely. -02-05 22:06:19

[Luzon/Provincial] I walked back home today drenched with sadness. The night sky was clear and beautiful. The brightest bodies reminded me of your smile, and their distance reminded me of how far I am from reaching them. I bought alcohol to quiet my soul. I sat in awe of their beauty, but the thought of ever reaching them humbled my feeble capacity as a human being. I had to delude myself to keep the tears from spilling. “Perhaps you’re the moon—bright, beautiful, perfectly composed. It takes a great deal of effort to reach you, but it isn’t impossible. Perhaps you’re Jupiter—larger, more radiant, and farther still. It would take generational talent to stand beside you.” These were lies. Because you’re the brightest thing I’ve ever seen, and celestial bodies other than stars cannot quite encapsulate you. A small light beside the brightest star only disappears. The brightest stars need an equally bright star to make something meaningful. It is self-deprecating to call myself the weakest light, but even knowing my potential, my radiance pales beside yours. And yet I’m stuck in a limbo. I’m drawn to you like a body tidally locked to something larger and condemned to circle, to watch, to want, to never quite arrive. I have to live with the pain of appreciating you from afar while aching to be closer. The only way this changes is if you turn your gaze toward me, and miraculously choose, somehow, to close the distance. -02-03 23:45:51

[Luzon/Provincial] There it is again—that funny feeling. Everything hurts all at once—physically, mentally, and socially. I’m aching. I don’t know where else to go, where else to vent. I love being alone, but I also love having friends, and being sociable is inevitable as a human being. Now that I’m here in UP, all my achievements mean nothing at best. Am I the only one who looks for a friend—a buddy, a person who matches my vibe and really wants to hang out with me? I’ve met a lot of people here: a person who is really kind but overstudies and doesn’t really match my energy; someone who really gets the vibes but seems like they don’t want to hang out with me because even when we’re together, they’re looking for someone else—and you can feel that they are nagsasawa with me; a person who gets me but is uncomfortable to be with and too uncommunicative; and a person who kind of matches my vibe but is too judgmental, too invalidating, and too suffocating to be with. Yet these are the four people I value the most here. But I don’t know how to keep being friends with them when being with them just hurts. I don’t want many friends. All I want is someone who gets me and will stick with me. My entire life, I’ve never had that friend. Always the funny one, but never the chosen one. What is wrong with me? I’ve always asked people what it is that they don’t like about me, but they don’t even say anything. I just want to know what to change—what to change to finally be chosen. I want someone to stick with me—a friend, not a lover. I’ve tried searching for someone like this, but no luck until now. It pains me because I thought my high school friends would be my dormmates, but in the end, they have their own circle. I was always an outcast—in friendships, in student council, in organizations, in every single thing. I’ve tried looking for that someone as a special someone, but I know that no matter what, the gaps inside me are looking for that one good FRIEND. Someone who will stick with me through thick and thin; someone to hang out with; someone to study with; someone to look for pogi/s with; someone to judge people with (the no-harm kind); and someone to cry with. My academics, leadership, talent, skills, and doubts in my course are killing me slowly. That’s why having no one—no friend—feels even heavier. I am jealous of those people who are really duos/besties -01-31 20:00:45

[Luzon/Provincial] The Painful Truth of Being a Pinoy Gen-Z: Mga Sikolohiyang Nakaugat Sa Likod Ng Aking Mga Pananalita Gamit Ang Ating Wika Kanina lamang po, ako po ay simpleng nakikipag-usap po sa aking sarili sa camera ng aking smartphone. So firstly po, I was giving myself a compassionate uplift as I've wronlgy judged my face recently—which I did po to help me reframe the negative thoughts that induced distorted insecurities po I thought on my face. The approach I did po was using the therapeutic skills I've learned so far from the free resources & guides online such as: mindfulness, interpesonal effectiveness (me to my reflection), and some others po na subconscious ko pong nagamit. Moving on po, I was suprised on how fast I was feeling relieved po in relation to myself during that self talk po (btw I used english po noon mainly to talk with myself sa camera). And then, as time passes by a little po, I suddenly thought po na what if I try to talk with myself naman po as much as I can using our pambansang wika naman po: Filipino. So ayon po, I started uttering Filipino words with what I can (🥹) kahit hindi po ako gano'n kagamay honestly sa wika po natin (and it's a shame on me na po na ngayon ko lang po na-realize 'yon dahil maybe sa Dunning Kruger effect na I feel 'confident' with my own Filipino proficiency even the way I spoke said otherwise *sigh*). So ayon po, going back to the topic,,, I DIDN'T EXPECT na bakit po parang nag-shift significantly 'yong feelings, thoughts, and imaginations ko po as I speak our own wika (btw I'm still looking eye-to-eye with myself po sa camera 😅). And ayon po, as I continued to speak our wika, I can see my thoughts flashing corruption, calamity crisis, superstitious stories, questionable 'Filipino Pride' movements where humility is na-silenced na po, and an overall apprehension na para bang napapasuko po ako bilang isang Pinoy na Gen-Z with this kind of awareness po at least. To be fair, the limitation naman po ng aking reflection ay ofcourse sa aking perceptions lang naman din po, which means based lang po ito so far sa knowledge ko in us Pinoys (yes, I'm also aware of the educational crisis reported by EDCOM II, which is very ano po,,, nakaka-agaw hininga alalahanin 😢, and I still believe in the reform's potency pa rin naman po.) To end this po, I sincerely wanted to share this post with all of you po kasi nakakalungkot na ganito po perception ko sa Pilipinas 😢, and I hope it's just a fleeting thought po for now where change is possible not just in our own thoughts, but also to where these kind of thoughts orignally came from din naman: the condition of our country. Ayon lamang po and looking forward for discussions po about this (I don't know po how to end this properly so ayon na lamang po ty 🥹.) -01-31 19:40:55

[Luzon/Provincial] Could be a lack of interest, but also maybe just different communication styles. After all, hindi rin naman basta-basta for someone to be able to feel comfortable enough to reveal things about themselves. If the vibes are ok naman pero the other person is really just not the type to ask questions, try sharing things about yourself related to the current topic. ("Natandaan ko tuloy yung ___<insert anecdote>___."). If wala pa ring follow-up questions even after several separate attempts, at least by then you can make a more informed decision whether to confront or stop whatever you have. From experimental-history(.)com/p/good-conversations-have-lots-of-doorknobs.. "Givers think that conversations unfold as a series of invitations [questions]; takers think conversations unfold as a series of declarations [straight up skl]. When giver meets giver or taker meets taker, all is well. When giver meets taker, however, giver gives, taker takes, and giver gets resentful (“Why won’t he ask me a single question?”) while taker has a lovely time (“She must really think I’m interesting!”) or gets annoyed (“My job is so boring, why does she keep asking me about it?”)." "What matters most, then, is not how much we give or take, but whether we offer and accept affordances. Takers can present big, graspable doorknobs (“I get kinda creeped out when couples treat their dogs like babies”) or not (“Let me tell you about the plot of the movie Must Love Dogs...”). Good taking makes the other side want to take too (“I know! My friends asked me to be the godparent to their Schnauzer, it’s so crazy” “What?? Was there a ceremony?”). Similarly, some questions have doorknobs (“Why do you think you and your brother turned out so different?”) and some don’t (“How many of your grandparents are still living?”). But even affordance-less giving can be met with affordance-ful taking (“I have one grandma still alive, and I think a lot about all this knowledge she has––how to raise a family, how to cope with tragedy, how to make chocolate zucchini bread––and how I feel anxious about learning from her while I still can”)" -01-28 16:21:18

[Luzon/Provincial] Sa lahat ng Cup of Joe song na maririnig ko, ikaw ang maaalala ko. When I was in senior high school, I had a suitor. He was not the ideal man na na-set ko sa utak ko noong bata pa ako, pero he set THE standard. It went well (more than well, actually), not until right after graduation. Pumunta siya ng Manila and cut off everyone close to him—kahit ako. This is because he wants to start a new life—bagong environment and everything. Nakapagpaalam siya nang maayos sa akin before siya umalis kaya naintindihan ko (aray lang kasi naiwan ako). But I understood him ‘cause I knew na kailangan niya ‘yon. If hindi niya iniwan ang buhay niya sa probinsya, mas lalo siyang mahihirapan makapag-adjust sa magiging bagong buhay niya sa Manila. Mini timelapse. After months of no contact, nagkaroon kami ng chance na makapag-usap ulit. Nagkikita pa kami almost monthly para mag-date at mag-catch up sa mga bagay na na-miss namin sa buhay ng isa’t isa. Paunti-unti, bumalik ang closeness naming dalawa. Kahit nga ‘yung spark ay present (which, sa side ko, ay never naman nawala). Siguro dahil hindi ko rin siya sinukuan talaga. For context, I kept in touch with his family noong mga panahong hindi niya ako kinakausap. Siya rin ang nagsabi na sa family niya muna ako makibalita. Nilinaw niya rin naman sa akin na ‘wag ko na siyang hintayin kasi may posibilidad na ang sa’min ay hanggang doon na lang talaga. Pero hinayaan ko lang siya. Hindi ko siya kinukulit o kahit na ano pa. Minsan naman ay nagpaparamdam siya kaya kapag gano’n ay para akong mapapatalon sa tuwa. Basta kung ano ang kaya niyang ibigay sa akin noong mga panahong ‘yon, more than enough na. Kaya noong nag-decide siya na gusto niyang bumalik sa buhay ko, hindi na ako nagpatumpik-tumpik pa. Tinanggap ko agad siya. ‘Yon din ang time na naging official ang relationship naming dalawa. Pero dito rin nagsimulang maging l*ch*-l*ch* na. Noong una, sobrang okay naman talaga. Mahal niya ako, at mahal ko rin siya. Para ngang hindi kami nagkaroon ng “no contact” kasi mas tumibay pa ang relasyon naming dalawa. Dito nga lang din nagsimulang lumala ang pagiging overthinker ko. Siguro dahil din kami’y magkalayo. Nasa Manila siya at nasa Elbi ako. Pero honestly, hindi kasi ako maganda—mukha man o katawan. Hindi ako matangkad, makinis, o maporma. Literal na simpleng babae lang talaga. Naisip ko, kayang-kaya niya akong palitan. Kung nagawa niya na akong iwan noon, kaya niya rin gawin sa’kin ‘yon ngayon. Mali ko na pinag-isipan ko siya nito. Mali ko na hindi ako fully nagtiwala sa kaniya. Pero ang totoo, sa sarili ko ako walang tiwala. Wala akong tiwala na kaya ko siyang bigyan ng rason para ang nangyari noon ay hindi na maulit pa. Kaya naman after ilang months namin maging official, hindi ko namalayan na pareho na pala kaming nagbago. Pinagdududahan ko siya sa mga bagay na ginagawa niya, at napapagod na rin siyang intindihin ang mga panghihinala ko. Ako rin kasi ang tipo na puro hinala, pero ‘di naman tumatama. Girl’s instinct? Wala ata ako no’n. Hindi naman din kasi siya ganoong tao—lahat ng assurance at update, naibibigay niya. Nagkakaproblema lang kapag nakakalimot siya, which is nagiging madalas na. Kaya naman palagi ko siyang sinasabihan na i-chat ako. Magsabi kung anong ginagawa o kung ano. Sa kauulit ko, napuno ako. Ayaw ko lang na inuutusan ko siya sa kung paano ako dapat itrato. Because of that, mas lalo ko siyang pinu-push na mag-explain sa akin, habang siya naman ay palayo na nang palayo. Hanggang sa pareho na kaming napuno. All this time, akala ko nawalan na siya ng care sa mga sinasabi ko. Na kaya siya nagiging avoidant ay dahil lang ‘yon ang kaniyang gusto. Hindi ko naisip na sa lahat ng mga sinasabi ko, hindi niya na nararamdaman na girlfriend niya ako. Puro kakulangan niya ang napapansin ko. Nalalampasan ko na pala ang mga pagkakamali ko. Hindi siya kagaya ko na kapag may napansing mali ay nagsasabi. Kaya rin siguro napagod siya’t ‘di na nakapagtimpi. Nang ma-realize ko lahat kanina habang magkausap kami, maling-mali nga talaga ako. Kaya naman sabi ko, aayusin namin at magbabago ako. Mali ko kasi hindi ko agad ‘yon ginawa; which in my defense ay hindi talaga ako aware na nagiging mabigat na sa kaniya—na kinikimkim niya na pala. Ang naisip na pala niya ay wala akong tiwala sa kaniya. Hindi niya na rin daw nararamdaman ang aking presensya. Akala ko kasi, everytime na sinasabi kong mahal ko siya ay kaakibat na no’n lahat ng salita—na may tiwala ako sa kaniya, na proud ako sa kaniya, na may care ako sa kaniya. Magkakaiba nga pala talaga ang mga salita. Kaya nga gusto kong i-prove sa kaniya na kaya kong magbago. Na babawi ako. Kami. Sa isa’t isa. Kaso huli na. Umayaw na siya. Hindi niya na raw ako mahal. Hindi na rin daw ako nakikita pang kasama sa future niya. Sinabi mo lang kaya ang mga ‘to para bitawan kita? Totoo nga kayang kahit kapiranggot ay wala nang natira sa loob mo na mahal mo ako? Kasi habang tinitingnan kita kanina, wala kang kahit anong ekspresyon sa mukha. Inamin mo rin naman na wala ka na talagang kahit na anong nararamdaman pa. Pero kahit sana man lang ngayon, tumama ang aking hinala: na nasabi mo lang ang mga bagay na ‘yon para i-let go kita, kahit ang totoo ay mahal mo pa rin ako. I hope magkaroon tayo ng peace na hindi natin nakuha sa isa’t isa, bu. Kung sakaling okay na, sana ay maituloy pa natin ang sa’ting dalawa. Sabi ko nga sa’yo kanina, basta sa’yo, hinding-hindi mauubos ang aking pasensya. I can never control your feelings, pero sana hindi magkaroon ng iba. Hindi tayo sina Popoy at Basha, pero baka kahit one more chance ay posible pa. Sana. Also, mali ako. Hindi lamang sa mga CoJ songs o sa kahit na anong kanta kita maaalala. Wala e. Mahal na mahal lang talaga kita. -01-27 17:02:04

Marami pa rin sigurong tahimik na humihiling na sana hindi sila mag-isa. Sana may kasama naman sila. Na sana may kasabay silang titikman ang mga tindang shawarma, kahit dumating sa puntong hindi na nila maintindihan ang pagkakaiba-iba ng kanilang lasa. Na sana may magyayaya sa kanila na sumakay ng rides kahit pa nakakatakot. Basta ang mahalaga, may kasama, kahit pwede silang masuka. Na sana may magsasabing "bagay sa'yo" pagkatapos mong pumili ng mga memorabilya at aksesorya. Na sana may kasabay kang sisigaw para makiisa sa panawagan ng masa. Na sana may kasabay kang umawit sa mga kantang matagal mo nang minemorya. Sa mga ganitong pagkakataon, huwag sanang agad sabihin ng mga tao na "pwede namang isama ang barkada," dahil hindi naman lahat may tropa. Huwag din sanang isipin ng iba na masyado silang uhaw magka-jowa, dahil maaring ang ilan sa kanila ay sawang-sawa nang mag-isa, malayo sa kanilang pamilya, o napag-iiwanan na. Ang sarap sigurong isipin na marami pa ring dadalo na may kasama. 'Yung ilan sa kanila may kaakbay at kayakap pa. Pero ang bigat lang isipin na marami pa ring mag-isa. 'Yung iba nga, kahit pa may kasama, humihiling pa ring may makilala. Kaya sa darating na Feb Fair, huwag sanang matakot ang kapwa ko isko at iska na yayain ang mga nagugustuhan nila. Malay niyo, naghihintayan lang pala kayo talaga. Sana magkakasama pa rin ang mga freshies at mga superclassmates nila. At para sa mga nangungulila, kapit lang, may makakasama rin kayo sa susunod niyong punta. At kung huling beses mo na ito, sulitin mo na. Hindi na mahalaga kung mag-isa. [Luzon/Provincial] Hi, OP from [Luzon/Provincial] here. Update: We broke up. Yes, guys. Nabasa niya yung post ko. Apparently, sobrang recognizable ng typings ko + super specific ng details. 😭 HAHAHA my bad. So ayun, after niya makita, we talked agad. And doon niya finally sinabi sa’kin lahat ng matagal na niyang binubble up, lahat ng mga kinimkim niya just to keep me. Inamin niya na kahit paulit-ulit niyang sinasabi na willing siyang mag-compromise, hindi raw siya 100% masaya sa idea. And honestly, ramdam ko naman ‘yon kahit noon pa. He really wants to have three kids. Not because traditional siya or conservative type (mas woke pa nga siya sa’kin HAHAHA), but because pangarap talaga niyang maging tatay. He grew up in a very loving family, bunso siya, spoiled in the best way by his siblings. He wants his future kids to experience the same kind of love he had growing up. Meanwhile, ako, panganay. Bata pa lang ako, parang nanay na agad. I’ve been taking care of people my whole life. And I know, deep in my bones, na ayoko na mag-alaga ng bata. Ayoko na ulitin ‘yon. Siguro eto talaga yung sinasabi nila na minsan hindi nagwo-work ang tandem ng panganay na babae at bunsong lalaki. Another thing: ayaw niya ng ingay kaya ayaw niya sa city. He’s a logical, calculated, practical guy. Meanwhile, ako? Gusto ko ng thrill. Gusto ko ng bago. And honestly, wala akong pakialam kung mapanghe, magulo, o mahal ang cost of living sa Manila. Matagal ko nang naririnig ‘yang mga warnings na ‘yan, pero never nila akong na-discourage. Kasi pangarap ko talagang maging city girl. I want to go as far as I can. From my family, from neighbors who wronged me, from the probinsyana life that deprived me of freedom and self-expression. From the kapitbahays na walang ginawa kundi mang-redtag sa’kin. I want to live somewhere where I actually feel like I’m living. Sobrang halo-halo ng comments sa first post. Pero at the end of the day, hindi rin naman ako sumunod sa kahit anong advice kasi ako na rin mismo yung nagdesisyon para sa sarili ko. HAHAHA. I chose to let him go. Kasi ayokong pigilan siya sa buhay na gusto niya. At ayoko ring pigilan ang sarili ko. Ayokong dumating yung araw na magkasama pa rin kami pero may silent na resentment na sa pagitan namin. The surprising part? Hindi siya kasing sakit ng inaasahan ko. Akala ko iiyak ako nang walang tigil. Yung tipong hindi makakain, hindi makatulog, parang guguho yung mundo ko. Pero hindi. I didn’t shed a tear. Kumain ako nang maayos. Buo pa rin appetite ko. Mahimbing tulog ko. I don’t know kung inuuto ko lang ba sarili ko, or baka sobrang fresh pa lang ng breakup (literal na days ago lang). Tinry akong i-cheer up ng friends ko, pero wala naman talagang kailangang i-comfort kasi okay talaga ako. Hindi ko rin masabing miss ko na siya, kasi technically, pwede ko naman siyang i-message anytime. We chose to remain friends. Ang hirap namang mag-no contact agad after 11 years. Sobrang biglaan ng changes para bigla na lang naming burahin ang isa’t isa. Lahat ng alaala ko, from pagkabata hanggang puberty, nandoon siya. Even if hindi na kami lovers, he’s still the person who knows me best. Siya yung naka-experience ng lahat kasama ko. Alam niya lahat ng nangyari sa buhay ko. Ang hindi ko lang alam ngayon ay kung paano sasabihin sa parents ko. Akala nila after grad, ikakasal na ako. Hindi ko rin alam kung paano haharapin ang parents niya, or kung paano ko sila bibitawan. They were so kind to me. Honestly, mas pamilya ko pa sila kaysa sa actual family ko. Pero kakayanin ko. I’ll be okay. And to R, if you’re reading this: I’m totally fine. Please don’t worry about me. Hindi mo na kailangang mag-reach out sa friends ko para tanungin kung okay lang ba ako (yes, sinabi nila sa’kin HAHAHA). I hope you’ll be okay too. “When the lights flicker, count to three.” IYKYK. -01-25 13:52:07

[Luzon/Provincial] In response to the UP campus rivalries I’ve been seeing online over the past few months, I can’t help but reflect on UP’s history. I hope you have time to read until the end. People often forget that the UP System was deliberately designed not to revolve around a single “main” campus, but to function as a network of premier universities across the country. In , UP President Salvador P. Lopez reorganized the university to promote democratization and decentralization, broaden access to quality education, and empower campuses nationwide. This restructuring was historically inspired by the University of California system, where multiple campuses operate under a shared vision while being encouraged to develop their own areas of excellence. From this perspective, UP was no longer meant to function as a single university with a main campus, but rather to draw strength from a system of universities. Notably, the UC System does not officially name or recognize any campus as its “flagship.” UC Berkeley, UC San [REDACTED_NAME], UC Davis, UCLA, and others are all promoted as premier institutions with distinct strengths. In UC governance documents, policies, and system-wide communications, campuses are presented as peers within a unified system. While some campuses may be older or more visible, the UC System deliberately avoids hierarchical labels. It’s true that public perception often assigns a “flagship” label to certain campuses. However, the important point is that the administration itself does not institutionalize this idea, as doing so would go against the vision of systemic growth. Even if the public informally labels a campus as a flagship, all universities within the system are treated as premier. Within UP, the continued use of the “flagship” narrative deserves reflection. While [Metro Manila]’s role and contributions are undeniable, the long-term growth of the UP System depends on ensuring that all constituent universities are equally supported. To be fair, there has already been visible progress in strengthening UP campuses in the regions over the years, but the scale and pace of support still need to match the system’s national mandate. Recent developments show why this discussion matters. In , UP Mindanao released a public statement expressing concern over significant funding constraints on their campus. Addressing these gaps is essential if UP is to fully meet its *national* mandate. Moving forward, there are constructive steps the UP administration (and national policymakers) could consider: • Moving away from hierarchical labels (e.g., “flagship”) in official documents and communications • Developing long-term plans to support the gradual or accelerated expansion of UP campuses in the regions and reassessing budget distribution among UP constituents. • Consistently promoting each constituent university as a premier institution • Further decentralizing governance and strengthening campus autonomy while reinforcing system-wide collaboration • Relocating the UP System’s Central Administration to a more neutral location, such as Bonifacio Global City or another UP campus (for context, the UC Office of the President is headquartered in Oakland for neutrality) • Expanding unique academic programs and allowing them to be adopted by more than one campus, for example, programs in journalism, urban planning, public health, or agriculture, so that access to specialized education can reach more regions These suggestions may be ambitious given existing budget constraints, but if UP is truly serious about its national mandate as a university system, this is the direction it needs to take. -alum15 -01-24 12:35:11

[Luzon/Provincial] Unsolicited advise sa mga fellow isko and iska. Wag na natin i-try na matapos ang kurso natin within 4 years or 5 years. Ok lang maging underload. Kung ano lang ang available na courses na maibigay sa atin, e di thank you. Ang ikakatapos ng ating kurso sa "saktong oras" base sa isang standard na hindi naman maibigay sa atin ng tama at maayos ay isang kahibangan. Gamitin na lang ang oras para matuto ng ibang bagay o mag part-time. Tutal, kung experience din lang naman ang kelangan daw sa labas, e di kumuha na ng experience sa labas or sa internet kung di rin lang mabibigay ng unibersidad dahil kulang ang teacher o classroom. Kapagod din mag-makaawa sa prerog na walang kasiguraduhang makukuha mo yung subject o yung oras na gusto natin. Papaka-uno ka nga para maganda grades mo pero di siguradong 18 units ka next sem para maganda track record mo. Sayang oras, sayang pagod, sayang pera. Mas ok na underload ka, di ka naghihikahos sa pagsabay-sabay na deadlines ng requirements na tinalo pa ang tatay mo sa trabaho pag may deadline sila, pero kumikita ka kasi may sideline ka or part-time job ka na dumadagdag pa sa isusulat mo sa resume mo pag gusto mo na mag-full-time, keysa sa "on-time" ka nga pero nagpapaka-ewan ka magmakaawa na makuha yung isang subject na "kelangan daw para sa semester mo" - pagkatapos e sabay sabay ang deadline, ang exam, na hindi naman ikadagdag sa resume mo kung may experience ka ba o wala pagkatapos mong grumaduate "on time". Minsan praktikal din magisip ang magulang ko kaya ok lang... matatapos din naman - di kelangan sumunod sa isang "standard" na hindi naman maayos ang pagbigay. -01-21 10:20:14

[Luzon/Provincial] What do I do in this situation? I have a boyfriend. We’ve been together since we were 12. Literal bata pa kami, tanga-tanga, hindi seryoso, and honestly just two curious kids na gusto lang masabi na may jowa. Walang ligaw-ligaw, walang grand gestures, basta na lang naging kami. We didn’t really know what we were doing back then. But growing up, we grew together. We learned what a real relationship actually meant. Somewhere along the way, naging seryoso kami, and we fell in love deeply. Naging kami right after elementary graduation. We literally watched each other go through every phase of life. Same schools, same SHS strand, parehong sa [Luzon/Provincial] nag-college (kahit magkaiba ng course). We’re 23 now, and we’ve been together for almost half our lives. He knows me more than anyone else in this world. He’s everything I could ever ask for. Never missed an important occasion. Never forgot to make me feel special. Always one call away. Alam niya exactly what to say when I’m overwhelmed or breaking down. Of course, nag-aaway din kami sometimes, pero never toxic, never disrespectful. He was, and still is, my best friend. We’ve always talked about the future. He dreams of having three kids and living a quiet life back in our province in Cavite. But I dream differently. I don’t want kids. I want to live in the city, maybe Manila. I want a bigger life, new places, new people. I want to leave the province behind. Cavite holds too many things that didn’t do me any good. Being a probinsyana isn’t the life I see for myself. We’ve talked about this so many times. And every time, he tells me he’s willing to compromise for me, for my dreams, for my happiness. But I see the sadness in his eyes, the disappointment he tries so hard to hide. He loves me enough to give up the life he’s always imagined. And I love him enough to wonder if that’s something I should even let him do. So now I’m stuck asking myself: Is it fair to stay, knowing our dreams don’t fully align? Should I let him compromise everything he’s ever wanted just so I can have the life I’m dreaming of? If I choose my dreams, am I giving up the best love I’ve ever known? If I choose him, will I eventually resent the life I didn’t get to live? -01-20 12:18:54

Grabe guilt tripping dahil lang sa hindi kami aligned sa panawagan nilang i-suspend ang block reg. Ang need niyan ay measures to improve the mechanism, never to abolish it now that we see the effects to the regular students. At saka, kuhang kuha na talaga pika namin ngayong laging biglaan ang abiso. Kita namin ang problema ng block registration, pero kita rin namin ang tulong nito. "Dagdag anx**ty at str**s ang block reg." Kahit alisin natin iyan, mas malala ang anx**ty. Hindi sa ganun dahil may laya tayong pumili ng preferred schedule natin. Matanong ko ibang kolehiyo, sa sistema ninyo, kahit mag-plot kayo ng schedule, hindi ninyo ito nasusunod. Ang laya ninyo ay limitado base sa natitirang slot o section. Nangangahulugan na ang laban dito ay sistematiko. Kakulangan ng budget, ng prof na magtuturo, ng mga specimen, ng facility. Hindi ito dahil sa block reg. Dahil sa block reg, may safety net. At kung babalikan natin ang problema ng mga irreg, mas makukuha nating linyahan ang petisyon. [Luzon/Provincial] Why So Few Poor Students Get In Let’s stop pretending. You’re not really teaching everyone. You’re teaching students who were already given a chance long before they stepped into the classroom. Why are there so few poor students admitted? Because if more of them got in, we’d be forced to face an uncomfortable truth: the system isn’t fair. If more students from poor families entered, we’d see what they’re actually up against—public schools with no resources, no review centers, empty stomachs, unstable homes, no books, no quiet place to study, no time. It would be impossible to keep saying that success is just about “hard work” or “talent.” That truth makes people uncomfortable. So we look away. When most of the students who pass come from well-off families, everything looks fine. It’s easy to say, “Pantay-pantay lang naman. Anyone can make it.” That story feels good—especially to those who already benefit from it. There’s another reason people don’t like to talk about: poor students need support. They need food. Housing. Internet. Learning materials. Guidance. Time. Care. Real help. And that costs money. When students already have these things, schools don’t have to do much. It’s easier. Cheaper. Less demanding. No pressure to fix what’s broken. So instead of putting money into student support, it goes into things that make money—corporate partnerships, campus malls, food chains, “development projects.” These are visible. Profitable. Safe choices. But students who struggle? They’re seen as a problem. As “too much.” As a reminder that something failed long before the exam. So the quiet choice is made: keep letting in those who can already survive. It’s easier to defend. Easier to manage. And it keeps the illusion alive—that the system is fair. This isn’t about lowering standards. Standards matter. But let’s be honest: merit isn’t neutral. You can’t separate performance from preparation, and preparation depends on money, safety, time, and support. Education was supposed to help people escape poverty. But when we only open doors to those who were already close to the door, we’re not fixing inequality—we’re hiding it. And that’s why so few poor students get in. Because when they’re kept out, no one asks hard questions. DepEd can keep failing—and stealing. State universities can avoid accountability. Other agencies can hide behind the idea that “everything is working.” As long as inequality stays invisible, corruption stays protected. -01-16 12:30:06

[Luzon/Provincial] Please don’t @ me; I’ve read extensively on this. I’m simply structuring my thoughts here, in case it benefits someone who needs it (call it charity for the greater good). The Indolence of the Filipino by José Rizal. Rizal never denied that “indolence” existed, he exposed how colonizers mislabeled rational behavior under oppression as laziness. When effort is not rewarded, when people are overtaxed, forced into labor, denied education, and punished arbitrarily, initiative dies. That isn’t moral failure; that’s structural violence. This isn’t abstract theory. As of , learning poverty in the Philippines was estimated at 91%. In PISA , only 24% of Filipino students reached minimum reading proficiency, versus 74% in OECD countries. Almost no one reached the level required for complex comprehension. What good are Rizal’s writings if most students are never given the conditions to truly understand them? That’s why the argument collapses when they say UP “doesn’t care if you’re rich or poor, theoretically.” Rizal would immediately ask: under what conditions was ability formed? Intelligence, grit, and capability are cultivated through access to quality education, stability, time, and resources, things that are not equally distributed. Rizal never argued for abandoning standards. He argued that merit cannot be measured honestly when conditions are unequal. A system that selects only at the end, without equalizing at the beginning, mistakes privilege for ability. That is the exact distortion Rizal critiqued. Yes, UP is bruising and unforgiving. But hardship is not experienced equally. Some students struggle only with academics; others struggle with hunger and survival on top of academics. Pretending all suffering is the same is intellectual dishonesty. On giving up the slot: That framing is a false dilemma. Rizal’s critique was never about personal sacrifice, it was about systems that confuse privilege with merit and then demand individual martyrdom instead of structural reform. UP is not a great equalizer. And that’s precisely why the argument can’t stop at “pare-pareho lang kayong nahihirapan.” Disparity outside the institution shapes outcomes inside it. Rizal would agree: no one deserves a slot just because they are poor. But “deserving” is meaningless without equal conditions. A fighting chance is not charity, it is the minimum requirement for merit to mean anything. Keeping the UPCAT is reasonable, if we admit it measures preparedness, not pure intelligence. Review centers help, but they only polish what already exists. If we stop there, we treat symptoms, not causes. Blaming individuals for “choosing to be inadequate” is exactly the colonial logic Rizal dismantled. Saying “kaya mo pa rin” while ignoring structural barriers is a convenient excuse not to fix the system. UP claims to form critical, radical thinkers for the nation. But service is not about romanticizing suffering or normalizing deprivation. Rizal warned against turning endurance into virtue while institutions evade responsibility. Honor and excellence mean nothing if service is demanded only from individuals while systems remain unjust. So yes, be a catalyst for change. But don’t reduce structural inequality to personal opinion, and don’t mistake survival within a broken system as proof that the system is fair. That kind of thinking is precisely what Rizal spent his life arguing against. Education gets P1.35 trillion budget, highest ever Source: -01-12 11:08:09

[Luzon/Provincial] An honest reflection on my experience with the student council. This isn’t just about the council itself, but also about the mass organizations closely connected to it. Over time, it became clear that they operate as one tight circle, supporting and understanding only each other. When you go against them, you’re quietly pushed out. Being a student leader is already demanding. It requires time, commitment, and personal sacrifice. Despite this, I don’t regret serving. What makes it difficult is the environment created by the people around you. There’s a culture of talking behind others’ backs. Once you no longer fit their idea of what an “activist” should be, you’re quickly labeled as performative, liberal, selfish, or individualistic as if there’s only one acceptable way to serve. I know some will read this and respond with the same labels. And yes, our country is facing far bigger problems—issues that demand urgency and collective action. But if the goal is to keep people involved, to sustain movements and participation, then at least be kind and humble. Stop looking down on others. It’s unfortunate, really. You are capable student leaders. Your critiques of the system are powerful, well-written, and necessary. [Luzon/Provincial] Alam niyo ba kung ano yung isa sa pinakaimportanteng wisdom na natutunan ko? Never let anyone dictate kung ano dapat mong maramdaman o gawin. We should stop forcing ourselves into molds that other people made, lalo na pagdating sa love. Gets naman na may standards, boundaries, at timing. Pero bakit parang ang daming rules? Ang daming kailangang sundin. “Huwag ka muna mag-reply.” “Paghintayin mo.” “Pahirapan mo muna—hard to get.” “Huwag mo munang sabihin nararamdaman mo.” As if kailangan nating ikulong ang sarili natin sa ganitong sistema para maging “tama” ang pagmamahal. But if you like someone, say it. Bakit mo pipigilan ang sarili mo kung hindi naman ‘yon ang totoo mong gusto? As long as alam mong wala kang nasasaktan, wala kang tinatapakan, at malinaw ang intensyon mo, bakit mo hahayaang ibang tao ang magdikta kung paano ka iibig? Ano naman kung ma-basted ka? Ano naman kung unrequited pala? Ano naman kung hindi nasuklian lahat ng effort, love, at sincerity mo? At the end of the day, ang mahalaga ay wala kang pinagsisisihan. Kasi naging totoo ka. Naging honest ka. You showed up as yourself, not as a version shaped by fear or other people’s expectations. Nakakatakot? Oo. Pero mas mabuti na ‘yon kaysa multuhin ka ng mga “sana ginawa ko” at “what if sinabi ko.” Love is already sufficient. You do things out of love, not for love. [Luzon/Provincial] Hi guys! Share ko lang pero a few weeks ago, nasa national museum of anthropology ako, kasama ko crushie (crushie pa ba yun? Kasi ate ko parang na skip na namin yung crush part eh if i literally cuddle with him) ko. Interests ko talaga ang history and sociology (which is odd na galing ako sa INSTAT), so while naglalakad kami sa mga galleries sa museum, I was explaining to him the purpose (gaya ng pagiging multi-purpose ng malong) and history of certain artifacts (like the Laguna copper plate) kasi some of them didn't have a proper description, so ako pa nag-eexplain yung purpose and symbolism of those objects. Nag-mala tour guide ang oat na ito dahil nga af** yung kasama ko. Ate ko, dami palang clout chasers sa national museum... di kami maka-sight see ng maayos kasi lahat nagpapapicture. Daming nakabalandra na mga magjojowa, di ko naman din masabihan ng sana ol kasi mukha naman din kaming mag boyfriend-girlfriend ni Tom (he's 4 years older than me). Di ako informed na need pala magdala ng boyfriend/girlfriend dito... While we were walking through the section nung gallery where they displayed pre-colonial Filipino writing (especially baybayin, but they also had other writing systems displayed), I suddenly started crying. Nakita ko kasi yung mannequin na babae na nakasuot ng sablay. The way the light shines sa telang naka habi at nakasabit sa kanyang balikat, she seemed like she was basking in the glory na finally she was wearing that maroon sash. Bigla akong napa-iyak. "That is what I dream to wear..." Wika ko sa kanya habang itinuturo ang sablay sa harap namin. He looked at it, smiled, and he saw na umiiyak na ako. He looked worried kasi paiyak na talaga ako nun, I told him, "I'm already delayed, Tom." Sinabi niya sa akin na, "that's okay..." While looking at me. Paano ako di maiinlove sa herodes na ito? Napaisip ako nung second na iyon na tama pa ba ginagawa ko? Is leaving the life I knew in Marikina really worth it so I can pursue my childhood dream of graduating in UP? Di lang yun, kasi sa totoo lang, the straw that broke the camel's back was yung kasama ko. He attended the university that I only dreamt of. Yung tipong I need to work 10x harder just to be able to secure a slot. Marami sa pamilya ko ang nakapag-tapos sa UP at yumabong ang mga karera sa kanya kanyang propesyon, kaya mula bata pa lamang ako, nakondisyon na ako na sa UP ako magko-kolehiyo. Mula sa pag-pasok ko sa isang science high school at pagpupunyagi ko doon upang maging honor student, hanggang sa pag-enroll sa isang review school upang makapasa ng UPCAT (yes, batch '24 ako) Kaso, nung high school ako, nag-iba ng konti ang ihip ng hangin at nais ko na makasama ang dati kong nobyo (hayss diyan tayo nadadale eh), 'di ko na pangarap itong unibersidad na ito 'eh. Kaya I chose programs na alam ko maraming may gusto kumuha, para di magalit sa akin magulang ko kung di man ako pumasa. As in wala na talaga akong interes nung panahon na iyon na pumasok sa UP. Kaso, ayon na nga, nag break kami nung kumag na iyon. So, last minute, needed ko na mag-aral. Nag lock in ako nung mga last weeks at kumapit sa diyos. At awa naman nito, ako'y andito. Kaso, I'm only here kasi I can't really afford to go to my dream university and sobrang daming kuskos balungos na requirements (daig pa UP) na I might as well just finish my degree in UP and maybe pursue KIT sa grad school. Wala na nga ako sa gusto kong program, ang dami ko pang naging sakit nung buong (that is another can of worms) that I basically f'ed up the entire semester. Singko ako sa majors ko and na probation ako this coming semester, and as people know, ladderized kami dito sa BS Stat so I'm cooked next semester unless maging math godz ako. Napaisip ako kasi high school me would be disappointed to see college me. Na damn grabe ang bibo kid ko nung high school, as in nag-uuwi pa ako ng medals from competitions and ngayon eto ako. Eto ako na isang burnt out na iska, puro tres ang grades. In fact, writing this piece made me remember my roots as a writer.—I'm a shell of the girl I used to be. Nung umalis kami ni babe doon sa parte na yun ng museo, I still couldn't avoid but think about my situation. Gusto ko man sabihin sa kanya yung nararamdaman ko, di ko naman masabi dahil di niya naman ako maiintindihan. Oo maiintindihan niya na delayed ako kasi siya rin (he didn't graduate on time either), pero maiintindihan niya ba yung mga pangarap ko and how important UP is for me? Di niya naman maiintindihan yun kasi he's not Filipino, which bums me out. - iskang pagod na at nais na lamang mag-asawa na (pls pls pls doon sa isa riyan baka naman) -01-10 13:35:55

[Luzon/Provincial] I am a graduate student (and I did not take my bachelor's degree here, I get your point. No need to shove it in my face). This coming semester will be my second. Before entering graduate school, I had a job that was unstable: uncertain, fragile, and never enough to promise a future. I stood at a crossroad: stay a little longer and endure, or resign and take the risk of further studies. I chose the latter. I walked away with nothing but the savings I had carefully built. By grace, I was later given the chance to receive a scholarship. It felt like a door opening just as I was about to knock on a wall. Many people see me as bright, idealistic, and persevering. Because of this, they assumed graduate school would be easy for me. But when my first semester began, I was overwhelmed by how everything worked. It was humbling. It shook me. It tested every part of who I thought I was. At first, last semester felt exciting. Then it slowly became suffocating. The deadlines piled up. The nights grew longer. Sleep became rare, replaced by anx**ty and the constant weight of unfinished tasks. My mind was crowded with one fear: failing (and being seen as a failure). I was under so much pressure, or at least that was what I told myself. Indeed, UP is not for the weak. Because of this, I was criticized by both peers and family, often without them knowing what I was truly going through. No one asked, “How are you?” or “Are you okay?” Instead, I heard, “Akala ko ba magaling ka?” and “Mag-aral ka kasing mabuti!” God knows I was trying. Second week of November. 2 a.m. A knife on my bedside. I was crying, convinced that ending everything would be the answer. But as I held it, my hand began to tremble. Panic rose in my chest. My breathing failed me. And then, nothing. I woke up from a failed attempt. It is already , yet the thought still haunts me. What if I had succeeded that night? How do I tell them that everything feels heavy right now? How do I tell them that I am trying? I am trying to survive the decisions I made for myself. I am trying to survive this war within me. -01-07 18:55:06

[Luzon/Provincial] After seeing so many posts in this freedom wall sharing personal stories of their s** life, I just want to share a few things I've learned as someone who has experience in that kind of stuff. Concerning lang kasi that some readers here may not have received genuine s** ed or don't have a trusted adult to guide them through it. 🍒 It’s sooo different from what you see in 🌽. I think one of the reasons why some are tempted to experience it for the first time is because of what they see in the media. In fact, it’s far from that. They show impulsivity, overconfidence, unrealistic scenarios na mapapa-(???) ka nalang. When in reality, there is hesitation and awkwardness (especially kung ‘di mo totally kilala yung tao na you’re doing it with). 🍒 Don’t engage with it if you feel even a small ounce of doubt about it. Don’t start something for the plot istg. If you really want to, be aware also of the dangers that come with it. There are things like HIV, AIDS, and other STDs that leave an impact in life. Plsplspls be safe and learn how to be safe when it comes to having s**. Some people also take advantage of innocent and naive individuals so watch out for those. If they are too pushy or don’t take no for an answer, be FIRM and DIRECT. ‘Wag mong isipin na baka ma-hurt feelings nila. Remove yourself from there IMMEDIATELY and don’t forget to block ✨. 🍒 I can’t stress this enough but CONSENT MATTERS!!! I don’t get why some people don’t know how to respect this. For the guys here, women pushing you away figuratively or literally DOES NOT mean they are playing hard to get!! They are uncomfortable and they don’t want you!! Accept that fact and move on. 🍒 In relation to STDs, always buy protection and don’t be afraid to get tested. In the first place, you do need to get tested prior to having partners or changing partners, both for your own safety and theirs. Some symptoms don’t appear immediately and only appear later on. If you’re going to do it with a person for the first time, it’s important to ask these questions: (When was the last time they had s**, did they use protection, and did they get tested). I’m not sure if the UHS has available testing kits for STDs, but I went to SAIL Calamba to get tested. They have many branches around Luzon and they don’t charge a fee (afaik). 🍒 The person you do it with matters. I don’t really recommend f*b*s and ons because the emotional and personal connection isn’t there. In my experience with ons, they weren’t really that memorable and it just felt like a waste of time tbh. Bottomline is, while s** can be an enjoyable and fun experience, it also has its downsides. It’s part of being a college student that you become exposed and aware of this stuff, and sometimes you are tempted to do so. But, education is important! These concerns arise from repeated lived experiences and are shared not only by students, but, I believe, also by Faculty-in-Charge (FICs). The current requirement for students to submit multiple “Plan A” and “Plan B” or "OPTIONS" schedules has proven to be counterproductive, especially for irregular students. In practice, it is common for one course under Plan A to be approved while the remaining courses are disapproved, forcing students to shift partially or entirely to Plan B schedules, or vice versa. This results in repeated revisions, schedule conflicts, and further delays in registration. Rather than resolving issues, the system generates confusion, inefficiency, and unnecessary str*ss for both students and FICs. This process has taken a significant toll on students’ m*ntal and emotional well-being. Personally, the anx**ty and str*ss associated with enrollment recur every semester, particularly after experiencing the same difficulties since the bloc registration system was implemented. These challenges are not exclusive to students; FICs are likewise burdened by repeated advising, schedule revisions, and administrative complications brought about by the current system. As a national university, it is concerning that the enrollment process remains highly inefficient and burdensome. While registration may never be entirely seamless, other universities have managed to implement systems that are considerably more organized and less str*ssful. No student wishes for an enrollment process that is unnecessarily difficult. While patience and resilience are values cultivated within this institution, students should not be required to repeatedly endure an outdated and excessively complicated system as a test of endurance. I would also like to str*ss that students should not be told to transfer to another institution simply because of enrollment difficulties. Students have the right to enroll in the University of the Philippines precisely because it offers a quality of education that other State, Universities, and Colleges may not. The issue lies not in the institution’s academic excellence, but in the inefficiency of the enrollment registration process. Although there are proper channels for raising these concerns, it has been deeply discouraging to observe that similar issues raised in the past have resulted in little to no improvement. This letter is therefore written in good faith, with the hope that it will not be dismissed as mere venting, but recognized as a sincere appeal for systemic reform. When students struggle, FICs are likewise affected, and the system ultimately fails both. With the reappointment of the current Dean, I sincerely hope that more student-centered and CVM-constituent-oriented policies will be implemented, particularly with regard to enrollment. The present system appears to favor regular students while placing irregular students at a disadvantage. It is important to recognize that irregular status is not always the result of negligence or poor academic performance. Many students face circumstances beyond their control, including health issues and serious personal situations. While students are constantly reminded to safeguard their units to avoid academic delinquency, it must also be acknowledged that not all delays or failures are within a student’s control. A more COMPASSIONATE, FLEXIBLE, and TRANSPARET enrollment system would better reflect the values of fairness, inclusivity, and excellence that the University of the Philippines upholds. I respectfully urge the CVM and [Luzon/Provincial] administration to review and improve the current enrollment registration process. A streamlined, transparent, and genuinely student-centered system would greatly benefit both students and faculty, and would align more closely with the standards expected of a national university. I sincerely hope that this message will lead to dialogue and meaningful action. Thank you for taking the time to read this letter. WITH ALL DUE RESPECT, PLEASE HEAR US! [Luzon/Provincial] To OP of Huwag kang magtaka, gayundin ang iba pang freshies na namomroblema, na ubos na agad ang HK sa unang araw pa lang ng enlistment. Nung freshie ako, nabigyan ako ng HK11 as preregistered pagpasok ko ng unibersidad. After nun, hindi na ko nakaswerte na makapagenlist sa HK12. Nakuha ko ang first HK12 ko nung 3rd year na ko, 2nd sem through prerog. Nagmakaawa ako sa fic na malapit na akong maggraduate pero wala pa din akong naeenroll na HK12. Yung 2nd HK12 ko, swertihan kong naenlist nung midyear ng 3rd year ko. At yung 3rd at huli kong HK12, naenlist ko last sem ko na. Sobrang takot ko dito dahil baka HK pa ang maging dahilan para di ako makagraduate. Hindi mo kailangang sundin ang curriculum mo kung kelan sinasuggest na itake mo ang HK. Kahit kailan ay pwede mo yang itake. So diskarte mo na yan kung kelan mo pagpaplanuhang ienlist. Isasabay mo ba sya sa mga mabibigat mong majors? Pero ikonsidera mong maige na by batch ang enlistment. Nagkakaubusan talaga ng HK dahil lahat ay kumukuha nyan. Bumubuti ang chances mong makaenlist ng HK habang tumatagal ka sa unibersidad. Mas nagiging maaga na kasi ang batch nyo sa enlistment schedule. Isa pang advice: Wag maging mapili sa HK. Yung mga popular na HK, obvious na madali yang maubos. [Luzon/Provincial] Happy New Year, FW. I’ve been in love with a friend for God knows how long now. I remember how this all started: we had been in the same class for the second time (and this was a few semesters ago at the time of writing.) While we were not particularly conversational the first time around, this time, I do know that we’ve been on each other’s radar. But this time, every end of class, I’d find myself talking to him, and I’d find every moment to linger—walk him to the places he’d need to be, and just prolonging every conversation we’ve had. But I knew it would be the last class we’d be together. By semester’s end, I asked him if he’d be down to hang out outside class. He was—and I know this because we did. It does not happen often, but we’d find ways to talk about our polar opposite fields and different axiological perspectives on life, but we’d converse. And I love every second of it. I knew that mixing romantic feelings with platonic friends are bad, so 1.) he does not know this, and 2.) given how busy I am, academic load and ancillary work, this romantic feeling was left to brew slowly. At the back of my mind, I know that I have to stop. In [REDACTED_NAME], I had written a piece of how I was letting him go. (see said post below). In [REDACTED_NAME], I was sure I had let that feeling go. But romance is stubborn. The feeling had not faded—it just took a backseat. And my horrible withdrawal post-Game Changer (book) series marathon is not helping—it had given me hope that at least in fiction, love can move mountains. That this feeling is worth the risk. That I am worth the risk. But reality calls—I still can’t let him know because I can’t lose him. Not when I’m at my final stretch of uni. Not when I can’t afford (literally and figuratively) to fail. Not when a single emotional paralysis can cost me my graduation and my admission in UP. I’m rambling on. Christmas and New Year passed, and I can’t bring myself to tell him, even if I really wanted to. So, I will leave what I have written last year. I hope would be better for me romantically. I hope it will for everyone. We all deserve sunshine, after all. *** APRIL : I love him. But I know this has to hurt. No, really. It has to. It has nothing to do with time, self-worth (or lack thereof), or self-dep**cation. In a world as hostile as this, even in the face of militancy, to love him is to let him go. To love him is to shield him from persecution. He deserves being loved out of the dark—his pure heart deserves to be celebrated across time and space. For him to have photos in public, with his hands held with pride and joy. For him to walk the Earth without unnecessary (and, in reality, unwarranted) cognitive processing, brought by the spotlight of deviance. Who I am is not what he signed up for. I can take a beating now and then, but to see him suffer makes it twice as hurtful. To love him is to know when to subject him to a lifetime of walking a tightrope. Who would he tick off by being with me? What would he set off on the people around him, as an unknown unknown become a norm? What could he take, and would he take it like I do? Where would he walk on eggshells, knowing previously that he could roam around carelessly? When would he realize that I am the causal link to the deviance he is charging straight toward? He deserves to live without cargo on his shoulders. To love him in this lifetime is to let someone else love him. The greatest sacrifice that love can give is to liberate them from a life of fear, doubt, and worry. As cheesy (or cringy) quoting a movie character, like Constance Wu, I would take pride in the future that when he marries the girl that he loves and enjoy the company of his grandchildren when he is old, it was because of me, as indirect as it could be. The highest form of love is to set him free. To love him is to let him go. -01-01 21:42:29

[Luzon/Provincial] Places to make kain: Bourgeoisie edition. Wala na ako sa LB so maglilista lang ako ng mga kainan na sa tingin ko ay magandang puntahan DTRI farm hub (100+) I recommend: The scenery, DTRI milk, coffee - I'm not normally the type of person to recommend a cafe solely because of the atmosphere and vibes. It's nice that they go through the effort of sourcing ingredients locally, but the food isn't anything remarkable. The view of [Luzon/Provincial] is absolutely beautiful though and goes well with a coffee. Vraja (100-150~ solo with student discount) I recommend: Vegan food Their food is very plain and absolutely not for everyone, but if you are sick of eating meat and don't mind plain veggies, it can be a very relaxing and convenient place to eat especially during more quiet business hours. Wildbreads (110+) I recommend: crossaint, baguette, bread - The best crossaint in LB, and you can get them fresh during the morning. If you are a bread person, you will like this place. Cynthia's (120+) I recommend: litson manok (120), ultimate burger steak (175) - They have decent servings and are open everyday including Sunday which is convenient. I mainly order their UBS. I want to say that whoever made the ultimate burger steak is a culinary genius. The combination of creamy gravy, french fries, egg and burger steak in one spoonful is divine. If you miss the UBS from J*llibee, look no further. The Burger Garage (150+) I recommend: Borgar, fries - There isn't a burger that comes close to their quality for the price. Smoky, juicy, and tender goodness. They certainly set the bar high for burgers at their price range. Sendongs (95+) I recommend: Empanada (95+), poqui poqui (210~), dinakdakan (230 solo) - Best empanada I've tried in LB, it's filling, and it has that wonderfully unhealthy and crispy crust. You have the option to add bagnet, cheese or make the empanada bigger, but I think their regular empanada (95) is already great. The solo bagnet (230) is expensive, but I like that the meat is dark and dry since it's more distinct from regular lechon kawali. Their poqui poqui for sharing (210~) is a must-try dish if you are a fan of eggplant like me. It's also the only place I've seen that offers authentic dinakdakan with pig brain. A lot of their food is for sharing, so bringing a group is a good idea. Boss D Ramen (300+) I recommend: tantanmen ramen (300~) - The ONLY good ramen I've had in [Luzon/Provincial]. Pricy but worth it. Their tantanmen is excellent and nutty. You can add an extra chashu or rice to their ramen for maximum enjoyment. They have other Japanese food in their menu too. The main con is it's near UPOU, so you either get it delivered, drive there or take a San Pablo jeep. Bittersweet Crumbs (120+) I recommend: Coffee, Hot Chocolate - BSC is my favorite coffee place. I order the cold brew regularly. They have a specific hot choco (190, I think it was sikwate? ) that I recommend if you want a hot choco that leans more on the cacao side which I strongly prefer over the usual sweet hot choco in cafes. You can have a filling meal here too, but I mainly visit for the drinks. Databites (210+) I recommend: burgers, any grilled meat, malagos dark chocolate, coffee - My other favorite cafe that I mainly go to for meals. Their main specialty is smoked meats, so I recommend anything grilled. They are pricy, but they do put effort in their dishes. My favorite is the loco moco (210) which is a hearty burger steak with gravy and potatoes. I've tried a lot of their solo meals, and they rarely disappoint in terms of quality. They have good coffee too, and their dark malagos hot chocolate (190) is another hot choco I recommend if you want something different from the usual sweet hot choco. . Cafe [REDACTED_NAME] (235+) Recommend: Tapa, Salpicao - Cafe [REDACTED_NAME] is pricy. Nevertheless, I love their beef tapa (235), it leans hard on the savory side (maybe 8/10 in savoriness), so the taste might be overwhelming for some. I also order their salpicao (270~) because it's the closest thing to their tapa which is regularly out of stock since they take time to marinade the tapa. I recommend if you want to try a very savory beef tapa. Seoul Kitchen (200+) I recommend: Korean Fried Chicken (195 one piece, 255 two piece) - I'm not big on Korean food, but I will list this place because this is my choice for the best fried chicken near campus. It's not a wings place like Mama Viol's or Stable, but the chicken is juicy and flavorful. I like their yangneom flavor the most, I found the orange and soy garlic to be surprisingly light in taste. Pizzeria Margherita (300+) I recommend: Pizza, pomodoro, carbonara - Authentic Italian food just a few steps away from the gate. The serving time might be long, but the wait is worth it. Even their basic margherita pizza (300~) is very worth the price. I like their 5 formaggi pizza and have also tried the pepperoni and ferrari, the pizza here does not disappoint. The pasta is no slouch either. Their pomodoro is good. It's also nice to try authentic carbonara that isn't the creamy white abomination that is common in this country. Their cheesy pastas like cacio el pepe are interesting because it's like eating a giant bowl of cheese. The only disappointing experience I've had on their menu is the aglio olio since I found the garlic to be overwhelming and bitter. In fact, you can look at their December 15 post of an aglio olio and see overcooked garlic. Aside from the aglio olio, I still highly recommend visiting the place. They also have a 20% student discount which is very generous, just bring your ID. IRRI Main Canteen (Price varies per dish, usually around 70-110~ for one ulam without rice, bringing 150-200 is a safe bet) I recommend: International food (~110), egg tofu (40) or anything with their green coconut milk-based curry, aloo gobi (~70), gochujang chicken (~110), kare-kare (~110, bagoong is average, but they use ox-tail) - If there is ONE place you should eat at before graduation, this is it. It is worth visiting here just for the experience alone. The scenery is great, IRRI is just a cool place to visit, and there's also the ridiculous amount of food choices available. I recommend visiting on a weekday because that's usually when they offer international food located at the right side of the canteen. If you want to be safe, ask the staff what they recommend. I usually grab Indian food, but sometimes I like to live dangerously and pick at random though note that not everything tastes good. There really is something for everyone due to the sheer amount of food choices available. Keep in mind that the menu is irregular, I've been eating here for almost two years, and new things still show up every now and then. One day it's Indian, the next day it's Italian. I've heard a complaint from an Indian student that their food isn't authentic because they lack spices which I agree with. If you are looking for truly authentic international cuisine, this is not the place to go to. I'm not gonna complain though authentic food is expensive. They have a lot of dessert options too like turon, bicho-bicho, buchi, etc. They also have unlimited soup. If you're still not satisfied after eating at the main canteen, the Beanhub cafe is just a few steps away with reasonable coffee prices. My only warning is that the main canteen can get very crowded on certain days, so if you arrive in the middle of the lunch rush or later, it might be hard to get a table, and the food might run out. From memory, they are open 7-7 on weekdays and 7-5 on weekends, but I really recommend trying to have lunch in IRRI on time and on a weekday. The main con is the place isn't the most convenient to get to unless you have your own wheels. -12-30 23:03:08

[Luzon/Provincial] This holiday doesn’t feel gloomy because I’m getting older, but because I’ve never felt poorer than I do now. Please don’t get me wrong. I’m grateful for everything I have this holiday—my family, my loved ones, and the good food on our table, things that many people don’t even have. Still, I find myself pitying my life as I spend the rest of my vacation in my room—bed rotting, doom scrolling, and watching my shows—while I see my friends receiving nice things and going out to different places. I can’t help but feel jealous of the experiences they get to have, the ones I know I won't be able to have. I never felt this way when I was a kid. Back then, I was happy just playing outside with friends. Now, I keep longing for something more. I don’t think it’s wrong to desire those things, either. We all want something greater for ourselves. Even the poor March sisters longed for these (iykyk), and I just get them. All I could do is wish and hope that maybe, sooner or later, I'll get to have nice stuff and experience those things too. And if you ever felt this way too, just remember that everything will be alright. Maybe it's not now, but it will. -12-30 19:56:12

[Luzon/Provincial] Bakit laging yung sisi ay nasa taong sumuko kaya umalis? Kapag napagod ka na, ikaw na agad ang mali. It shows in who chose to listen when change was needed. Sinabi na kung ano ang masakit, kung ano ang hindi na tama, kung ano ang kailangang ayusin, pero walang nagbago. Hindi lahat ng umaalis ay mahina. Minsan, umaalis ka kasi wala ka nang matira sa sarili mo. Kasi ang pag-alis na lang ang paraan para hindi tuluyang maubos. Hindi mo pwedeng ipaglaban ang isang relasyon habang ikaw ang unti-unting nawawala. Kung may umalis, tanungin muna kung bakit. Kung may napagod, tanungin kung sino ang ayaw makinig. [Luzon/Provincial] Since it’s the season of change (New Year) is coming, I pasted here a part of a diary entry from a day this December: It’s the nth of December and I still feel this looming sense of guilt and holiday melancholy that, I’m sure, not only me is having this season. I haven’t been doing much activities lately. My family isn’t really the “celebratory” type. I belong to the family category where we would just stay at home, do chores, and live like how we’ve been living for the rest of the year— indifferent of the way the rest of the world’s celebrating right now. It’s honestly kind of sad to not have grown up in a family opposite to ours. But what can I do? At least it’s not too late to change things. Now that we have kids at home (my nieces), we’re trying to change things up a bit to make them have a different experience. As simple as they may be, living in moments when you’re a kid feels a lot more magical and eventful than when you’re a grown up. I don’t want to make this too long, but I just want to address how much I’ve changed throughout the year— only noticing some parts of me that I’m now proud about— and learning more of myself, from the littlest to the grandest degree of self-awareness. I recently learn that to actually transform yourself to an entirely new person— more disciplined, more confident, kinder, etc.— sticking on a constant routine, buying self-improvement books, or waking up at 5 AM every morning wouldn’t be enough. These are all external factors that may crumble to pieces once we subconsciously get back to our old ways. What we need, according to that one article, is a change in identity— a metamorphosis of our inner subconscious and beliefs of who we are as a person. Don’t say “I will” and instead say “I am.” You ARE what you WILL become. Manifest it early on, make it your identity, and maybe, once and for all, you’ll finally be what you’re always meant to be. Happy New Year... and Happy New You! 🌷 -12-28 23:19:46

[Luzon/Provincial] ‎Lately, I’ve been hearing a lot of people warning others not to become performative activists. I get the idea na activism should be deeply rooted, principled, and not reduced to aesthetics or clout. But honestly, the way this is often said feels less like a critique and more like a veiled attack. ‎ ‎What bothers me is this: regardless of whether someone is “performative” or not, they are still showing up, still speaking out publicly, still risking backlash and danger. That already means something... so why does it feel like activists are more eager to police each other than to confront the systems they claim to oppose? Bakit parang kayo-kayo lang din ang nagbababaan? ‎ ‎And genuine question, how exactly do you decide that someone is performative? Do you know their intentions? Their process? Their history? Or is it enough that they don’t meet your personal standard of what a “real” activist should look like? ‎ ‎I want to understand where this narrative is coming from, because from where I’m standing, it often feels like moral gatekeeping disguised as political consciousness. As if activism has turned into a purity test, where one wrong move, one imperfect expression, and you’re immediately dismissed. ‎ ‎I’m not an activist, I’m mostly quiet. But even from the outside, it’s hard not to notice how aggressively this label is thrown around, like these “performative activists” are everywhere, and like shaming them somehow strengthens the movement. If anything, it feels like it does the opposite. ‎ ‎Also, to be honest, I’m somewhat worried about how the label of “performative activism” is being used. Dati, madalas ko lang naririnig yung akusasyong “performative” lang kayo sa pinaglalaban niyo from people who are openly anti-activism. Sana hindi ito umabot sa point na nakaka-discourage sa mga bagong aktibista o sa mga gustong mag-step out of their comfort zones at matuto, kasi mas mahalaga ang participation at growth kaysa perfection sa simula. ‎ ‎I hope this gets posted, not to provoke, but to invite conversation from different perspectives and to better understand how others see this issue. -12-28 20:50:21

[Luzon/Provincial] i am so sick and tired of the difficulty of finding housing here in [Luzon/Provincial]. First, vetmed is a very notable course here and it makes sense na vetmed = kailangan magdala ng hayop for certain subjects. cats, dog, maybe a chicken, or rabbit, or what. Sure, maybe you can get away learning vetmed without having to bring animals/have a personal pet. But this is a clear example of like, this isn’t just about privilege–this is a course that literally requires the handling of animals. Then majority of housing choices here don’t even ALLOW pets. Maybe some for a few nights at most. That alone is such a huge stressor for students. Can we please make this make sense? What if kailangan alagaan ang pasyente for at least a week? Do they still have to beg the landlords for all this? I can understand wanting to not have to deal with the liabilities that come with having animals in a rented property. But so many posts on housemates are consistently looking for pet friendly spaces, regardless if theyre dvm or not, I feel it should be a bit more considered given the nature of at least one course here. Second. What the actual f** is up with the conditions of the housing. I’ve been apartment/dorm hunting for more than two sems. 4k hihingi niyo for 4 people in a room with only one desk. 5k for a whole unit, pero the entire bathroom ceiling is covered in BLACK MOLD. Apartments with literally just one window. Apartments with absolutely no windows. Nakakagalit. I have family in manila who pay LESS for a decent condo unit than what people in elbi are paying for literally just a room thats shared with how many people. Hindi naman sobrang OA hinihingi ko. Pero napapaisip naman din kasi ako, sure, hindi ko kukunin ang mga units na hindi pasok sa standards ko. Pero the thing is someone else will. And nalulungkot akong isipin na, sure, di ko kukunin ang apartment na sobrang run down, panget, walang sapat na ventilation, puno ng amag, dinadaga, iniipis ng malala, wala man lang flush ang toilet, di secure—but someone else would. And minsan naman talaga di nila choice and wala na silang options. Pero I do think of the people who will end up there. Third, the scammers. I don’t know what possesses these people to scam STUDENTS. Nabiktima na nga ako. Marami din akong nakikitang scam posts. Una sa lahat di na nga okay mag scam, tas ang mga iscscam mo pa mga estudyante. Apaka gago nila, wala man lang konsensiya. We really need to start speaking up, or there has to be something done about all of this. Sobrang pagod na pagod na ako maghanap, makakita ng scam, madisappoint sa conditions ng housing, and think of the person who will be unfortunate enough to end up there. nakakapagod. -12-26 18:36:51
